target.title: fix(tts): deliver TTS media in message_tool_only contexts
target.body:
## Summary

- Dynamic TTS tool calls in Discord/Telegram group chats succeed and stage audio in `media/outbound`, but the channel send is never invoked because `shouldSuppressProgressDelivery()` blocks all delivery when the mode is `message_tool_only`.
- The tool result delivery path in `dispatch-from-config.ts` ran TTS processing (`maybeApplyTtsToReplyPayload`) AFTER the `shouldSuppressProgressDelivery()` early return, so TTS audio payloads were never created and never delivered.
- Moved TTS payload processing before the suppression check and added a media exemption: when the processed payload contains outbound media (TTS audio), delivery proceeds even in `message_tool_only` mode; text-only tool results remain suppressed.

### What did NOT change

- No changes to TTS synthesis, audio encoding, or the `maybeApplyTtsToReplyPayload` function.
- No changes to `suppressAutomaticSourceDelivery` or the source reply visibility policy.
- No changes to non-TTS tool result delivery behavior.

## Change Type

- [x] Bug fix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue)
- [ ] New feature (non-breaking change which adds functionality)
- [ ] Breaking change (fix or feature that would cause existing functionality to not work as expected)
- [ ] Refactoring (no functional change)
- [ ] Documentation update
- [ ] CI/Build change

## Scope

- [x] Reply dispatch / TTS auto-delivery
- [ ] Core messaging/outbound
- [ ] Plugins
- [ ] CLI
- [ ] Channel adapters
- [ ] Configuration

## Root Cause

In `dispatch-from-config.ts`, the `onToolResult` callback for the tool result delivery path had the following order:

1. Check `shouldSuppressProgressDelivery()` → return early if true (line 1928)
2. Process TTS payload via `maybeApplyTtsToReplyPayload` (line 1931)
3. Deliver the processed payload

In `message_tool_only` mode, `suppressDelivery = true`, so `shouldSuppressProgressDelivery()` returned true, causing the early return at step 1. The TTS processing at step 2 never ran, and the audio was never delivered.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** TTS auto-delivery now proceeds in message_tool_only contexts when the tool result contains outbound media (TTS audio).

**Real environment tested:** Node 22.x on Linux, production source via unit tests.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

    node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts
    node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.reply-dispatch.test.ts

**Evidence after fix:**

    dispatch-from-config.test.ts: 138 passed (138)
    dispatch-from-config.reply-dispatch.test.ts: 4 passed (4)

**Observed result after fix:** All 142 existing dispatch tests pass. The code change is minimal: TTS payload processing is moved before the suppression check, and a `ttsMediaExempt` flag allows media-bearing payloads through while keeping text-only tool progress suppressed in message_tool_only mode.

**What was not tested:** Live TTS synthesis with a real Discord/Telegram channel. The delivery path change is verified through the existing comprehensive dispatch test suite that covers message_tool_only mode and tool result delivery.

## Regression Test Plan

- [x] `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts` — 138/138 pass
- [x] `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.reply-dispatch.test.ts` — 4/4 pass
- [ ] Manual: configure Discord/Telegram group chat with TTS enabled, invoke tts tool, verify audio is delivered

## User-visible Changes

Discord and Telegram group chat users will now receive TTS audio when the agent invokes the dynamic `tts` tool. Previously, the audio was synthesized but never sent to the channel.

## Security Impact

No security boundary changes. The fix only affects the delivery decision for tool result payloads that contain media. Text-only tool results remain suppressed in message_tool_only mode. No changes to auth, credential handling, or access control.

Closes #83636

target.title: fix: preserve internal handoff status attribution [AI-assisted]
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: internal inter-session handoffs such as `subagent_announce` can run on the requester Telegram topic session and, after fallback, overwrite the same model/fallback/usage fields that `/status` presents as user-facing session state.
- Solution: make run/session usage persistence provenance-aware so internal handoff runs preserve the user-facing session model, context, usage, cost, and fallback notice fields.
- What changed: shared the agent-mediated completion source-tool vocabulary with Peter's #85089 subagent completion handoff path, then applied that provenance at the parent/requester session accounting boundary.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): this does not reintroduce child-side `message` tool delivery or change the native subagent completion handoff behavior from #85089.

## Motivation

Fixes the concrete Telegram topic confusion in https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/85082: an internal announce/recovery turn can use `google/gemini-2.5-flash` fallback while the normal topic session remains `openai-codex/gpt-5.5`, but `/status` and activity can make the topic look like it switched to Gemini.

The implementation is intentionally in the same direction as https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/85089: subagent completion stays mediated through the parent/requester session, and this PR only prevents internal handoff provenance from being recorded as the user-facing topic model/status state.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [x] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [x] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #85082
- Related #85089
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Internal subagent announce/recovery or agent-mediated completion turns that use fallback no longer replace the requester topic's user-facing model/fallback/usage state shown by `/status`.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout on Windows 11, Node 24.13.0, branch `fix/issue-85082-status-provenance`; plus reporter's live OpenClaw VPS Telegram instance running OpenClaw 2026.5.19 with this PR temporarily applied to the built `dist` files for proof capture.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  1. In Telegram forum topic `telegram:group:-1003871627242:topic:6823`, the reporter sent a prompt requiring `sessions_spawn` with `runtime="subagent"`, `agentId="codex"`, `context="isolated"`, `mode="run"`, `cleanup="delete"`, and `runTimeoutSeconds=120`.
  2. The child task was to reply exactly `PR85726 child subagent OK`.
  3. The parent used `sessions_yield` to wait for completion.
  4. After the internal completion handoff, the reporter ran `/status` in the same Telegram topic.
- Evidence after fix:
  - Gateway log accepted the spawn and wait: run `65a76f01-dfb2-4125-a0f1-07d80668f0aa`, child session `agent:codex:subagent:de83144a-492b-41e4-8820-effd80752265`.
  - Child Codex harness session `db366f78-a600-4237-b311-739053d8d331` ran under `openai-codex/gpt-5.5` and returned exactly `PR85726 child subagent OK`.
  - Parent transcript then received an inter-session message with `sourceTool=subagent_announce`, `sourceSession=agent:codex:subagent:de83144a-492b-41e4-8820-effd80752265`, task `reply exactly "PR85726 child subagent OK"`, and child result `PR85726 child subagent OK`.
  - The parent processed that internal announce run as `openai-codex/gpt-5.5` and returned `NO_REPLY`; this exposed a separate visible-delivery behavior already in the #85089/Peter handoff area, but it did not contaminate the topic's status attribution.
  - Backend session store after the handoff remained `modelProvider=openai-codex`, `model=gpt-5.5`, `lastTo=telegram:-1003871627242:topic:6823`, with no fallback notice fields set.
  - Reporter-provided `/status` screenshot from the same Telegram topic after the handoff showed: `Model: openai/gpt-5.5`, `Fallbacks: google/gemini-2.5-flash` configured, `Session: agent:main:telegram:group:-1003871627242:topic:6823 ? updated just now`, and `Execution: direct ? Runtime: OpenAI Codex`.
- Local regression commands run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts src/sessions/input-provenance.test.ts --reporter dot`; `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.auto-reply-reply.config.ts src/auto-reply/reply/session.test.ts --reporter dot`; `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.auto-reply-reply.config.ts src/auto-reply/reply/followup-runner.test.ts --reporter dot`; `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.e2e.config.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner.runreplyagent.e2e.test.ts --reporter dot`; `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.auto-reply.config.ts src/auto-reply/status.test.ts --reporter dot`; plus focused `src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.test.ts` cases for #85089's parent handoff path.
- Local evidence after fix: `src/sessions/input-provenance.test.ts` 18 passed, `src/auto-reply/reply/session.test.ts` 88 passed, `src/auto-reply/reply/followup-runner.test.ts` 46 passed, `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner.runreplyagent.e2e.test.ts` 51 passed, `src/auto-reply/status.test.ts` 74 passed, and focused `src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.test.ts` handoff cases 5 passed / 54 skipped. `oxfmt --check`, `oxlint`, and `git diff --check` also completed with no output/errors.
- Observed result after fix: In the live Telegram run, the real Codex harness subagent completed and the parent `subagent_announce` handoff did not rewrite the requester topic status. Same-topic `/status` after the handoff showed `Model: openai/gpt-5.5`, `Session: agent:main:telegram:group:-1003871627242:topic:6823 ? updated just now`, and `Execution: direct ? Runtime: OpenAI Codex`; the backend session store matched `modelProvider=openai-codex`, `model=gpt-5.5`, with no fallback notice fields set. In local regression tests, the forced internal Gemini fallback case preserves the requester session state.
- Fallback-specific guardrail: The new regression coverage seeds a user-facing `openai-codex/gpt-5.5` requester session, simulates an internal `subagent_announce` fallback to `google/gemini-2.5-flash`, and confirms the requester session keeps its visible model, context, token/cost accounting, and fallback notice fields instead of showing the internal Gemini run as the topic state.
- What was not tested: I did not deliberately break or slow OpenAI/Codex on the reporter's live install to force a Gemini fallback during the proof run. The live VPS proof exercises the real Codex harness subagent plus `subagent_announce` handoff path; the forced-fallback case is covered by targeted regression tests.
- Before evidence: Issue #85082 includes redacted live VPS trajectory/log output showing internal announce run `announce:v1:agent:codex:subagent:c34fca91-...` using `google/gemini-2.5-flash` with about 40k/122 tokens while a later normal topic turn returned to `openai-codex/gpt-5.5`.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: `subagent_announce` and adjacent internal handoff turns carry `inputProvenance`, but the reply/session accounting path treated their fallback provider/model/usage as ordinary requester session state.
- Missing detection / guardrail: There was no regression asserting that internal inter-session provenance must preserve user-facing session model/status fields.
- Contributing context (if known): #85089 simplified subagent completion delivery by keeping completion handoff mediated through the parent/requester. That leaves status attribution as the right boundary for this issue.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [x] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `src/sessions/input-provenance.test.ts`, `src/auto-reply/reply/session.test.ts`, `src/auto-reply/reply/followup-runner.test.ts`, `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner.runreplyagent.e2e.test.ts`.
- Scenario the test should lock in: an internal `subagent_announce` fallback to Gemini must not rewrite the requester topic's visible OpenAI/Codex model, fallback notices, context, token usage, or estimated cost.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: it exercises the provenance, direct reply-run, queued followup, and persisted session usage boundaries without needing live Telegram credentials.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): existing status/fallback tests covered ordinary user-facing fallback, but not internal handoff attribution.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

`/status` and activity state should no longer make a Telegram topic look like it switched to an internal fallback model merely because a subagent announce/recovery handoff used that fallback.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[subagent announce fallback] -> [requester session usage fields] -> [/status shows internal fallback as topic state]

After:
[subagent announce fallback + provenance] -> [preserve user-facing requester state] -> [/status keeps topic model attribution]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Windows 11 local Codex checkout; reporter live OpenClaw VPS for Telegram proof
- Runtime/container: Node 24.13.0 locally; live OpenClaw 2026.5.19 on VPS with temporary built-dist patch for proof
- Model/provider: OpenAI Codex primary with Google Gemini fallback attribution
- Integration/channel (if any): Telegram forum topic `telegram:group:-1003871627242:topic:6823`
- Relevant config (redacted): fallback provider configured as `google/gemini-2.5-flash`; secrets omitted

### Steps

1. Seed a requester session as `openai-codex/gpt-5.5`.
2. Run an internal inter-session `subagent_announce` path whose actual runtime metadata reports `google/gemini-2.5-flash` fallback.
3. Persist usage/fallback state and inspect the session entry/status-facing fields.
4. On the live VPS, spawn a real Codex harness subagent from the Telegram topic and observe the internal `subagent_announce` handoff.
5. Run `/status` in the same Telegram topic after the handoff.

### Expected

- The requester session keeps user-facing model/status attribution for `openai-codex/gpt-5.5`.
- Internal handoff fallback usage does not write the topic's visible fallback notice/token/cost fields.
- After the live Codex subagent handoff, `/status` still presents the requester topic as OpenAI/Codex rather than Gemini.

### Actual

- After this patch, local regression tests confirm the expected preserved state for the forced-fallback case.
- Live VPS Telegram proof confirmed the real Codex harness subagent and `subagent_announce` path, and `/status` after the handoff still showed `Model: openai/gpt-5.5` for the same topic session.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [x] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: provenance classification, direct reply-run fallback preservation, queued followup preservation, status rendering coverage, #85089-style parent handoff source-tool classification, and a live Telegram forum-topic proof using a real `agentId="codex"` subagent.
- Edge cases checked: heartbeats still preserve only displayed model while keeping their usage updates; ordinary user-facing fallback notices still persist and render; generated media source tools still use the parent/message-tool evidence logic from #85089.
- What you did **not** verify: I did not deliberately force provider failure on the live VPS; fallback-specific preservation is covered by regression tests.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: Hiding internal handoff accounting could make it harder to inspect internal fallback usage from the normal session status card.
  - Mitigation: the change preserves user-facing session state only for explicit internal/agent-mediated provenance; ordinary user-facing runs and heartbeat usage behavior remain covered by existing and new tests.

target.title: Browser: harden noVNC bootstrap headers
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: the loopback-only `/sandbox/novnc` bootstrap page relied on `no-store` and `no-referrer`, but it did not send a document CSP, `nosniff`, or frame-deny headers.
- Why it matters: this page carries the short-lived observer redirect flow, so it should be locked down as tightly as possible even though the route is loopback-only and token-gated.
- What changed: generate a per-response script nonce, attach a restrictive CSP, and add `X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff` plus `X-Frame-Options: DENY`.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): the existing one-time observer token URL flow and noVNC redirect behavior stay the same.

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor
- [ ] Docs
- [x] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [x] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #42476

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

None.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation:

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS
- Runtime/container: Node 22 + Vitest
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): Browser bridge / sandbox noVNC observer
- Relevant config (redacted): loopback browser bridge with auth token and short-lived noVNC observer token

### Steps

1. Start the browser bridge with `resolveSandboxNoVncToken` enabled.
2. Request `/sandbox/novnc?token=<valid-token>`.
3. Inspect response headers and bootstrap HTML.

### Expected

- The bootstrap page still redirects to the local noVNC target.
- The response includes no-store/no-referrer plus strict document hardening headers.
- The password remains in the URL fragment, not the query string or `Location` header.

### Actual

- The response now includes a nonce-based CSP, `X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff`, and `X-Frame-Options: DENY` while preserving the existing redirect flow.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: `/sandbox/novnc` still serves bootstrap HTML, still embeds the target noVNC fragment URL, and now emits the expected CSP / frame / nosniff headers.
- Edge cases checked: the CSP nonce in the header matches the nonce attached to the inline bootstrap script; token helper tests still pass.
- What you did **not** verify: end-to-end browser navigation in a real noVNC session.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself. Do not leave bot review conversation cleanup for maintainers.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps:

## Failure Recovery (if this breaks)

- How to disable/revert this change quickly: revert commit 4cf57794f1b6c1b3aa1bf7fd0d22ea842c45da36.
- Files/config to restore: `src/browser/bridge-server.ts`, `src/browser/bridge-server.auth.test.ts`
- Known bad symptoms reviewers should watch for: `/sandbox/novnc` returns 200 but the bootstrap script is blocked by CSP, or headers regress to being absent.

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: an incorrect CSP could block the bootstrap redirect script.
- Mitigation: the test now asserts that the per-response nonce is present both in the HTML and the header, and the redirect body content is unchanged.

target.title: fix(auth-profiles): canonicalize legacy default oauth session override
target.body:
## Summary

Describe the problem and fix in 2–5 bullets:

- Problem: sessions with `authProfileOverride=provider:default` could remain pinned to the legacy default id even when a canonical oauth alias (for example `provider:user@example.com`) existed.
- Why it matters: override drift makes profile identity less durable and can cause confusing auth/profile behavior across compaction/session lifecycle.
- What changed: `resolveSessionAuthProfileOverride` now canonicalizes legacy default ids to preferred oauth aliases (including fallback preference for non-`*:default` oauth aliases), with cooldown-aware selection.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): no transport/auth provider protocol changes, no new permissions, no config schema changes.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [x] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Auto session overrides that were previously stored as `provider:default` now resolve to canonical oauth aliases when available, reducing legacy-default persistence.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation:

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux (Arch)
- Runtime/container: local dev checkout
- Model/provider: N/A (unit tests)
- Integration/channel (if any): N/A
- Relevant config (redacted): auth profile store fixture with legacy default + oauth aliases

### Steps

1. Create auth profile store containing `openai-codex:default` and non-default oauth alias entries.
2. Set `sessionEntry.authProfileOverride = "openai-codex:default"` with source `auto`.
3. Call `resolveSessionAuthProfileOverride(...)`.

### Expected

- Resolution prefers canonical non-default oauth alias where available.

### Actual

- After patch, resolution returns alias (`openai-codex:josh@example.com` or fallback oauth alias) and updates session override accordingly.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

Local verification:

```bash
pnpm vitest run src/agents/auth-profiles/session-override.test.ts
# PASS: 3 tests
```

Added regression coverage:
- canonicalizes legacy `:default` auto override to email profile when available
- prefers non-default oauth alias even when alias email metadata is missing

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios:
  - Legacy default auto override canonicalizes to oauth alias.
  - Fallback alias selection works without alias email metadata.
  - Existing test behavior remains green.
- Edge cases checked:
  - Cooldown-aware candidate selection remains respected by resolver flow.
- What you did **not** verify:
  - Full cross-provider matrix beyond `openai-codex` fixtures.
  - End-to-end multi-session rotation under production load.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself. Do not leave bot review conversation cleanup for maintainers.

target.title: feat(agents): expose estimated context budget status
target.body:
## Summary

Expose OpenClaw's pre-prompt context budget estimate when fresh provider usage is unavailable.

- capture a typed, path-free `contextBudgetStatus` snapshot from the pre-prompt estimate
- persist that snapshot on `SessionEntry`
- expose it on gateway session rows
- let `/status` show estimated context usage only when fresh provider usage is unavailable or absent
- keep positive fresh `totalTokens` authoritative
- prevent preserved-runtime heartbeat runs from replacing the main session's budget snapshot
- clear stale `contextBudgetStatus` on runtime refreshes without a current estimate, model override/reset, manual/native CLI compaction, reply-run reset, and `/new` session rotation
- reserve the new top-level session field from plugin session-entry slot mirroring

This is reporting-only. It does not change compaction thresholds, pruning, prompt-building, model routing, or payload policy.

Refs #80594, #54996, #77992, #84490, #83177, #43009, #83526, #8635.

## Verification

- `git diff --check`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/session.test.ts -t "drops cached skills snapshot when /new rotates an existing session" --reporter=verbose`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/command/session-store.test.ts --reporter=verbose`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/status.test.ts -t "estimated context budget" --reporter=verbose`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server.sessions.compaction.test.ts src/sessions/model-overrides.test.ts --reporter=verbose`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-session-reset.test.ts -t "rotates the session and clears stale runtime and fallback fields" --reporter=verbose`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/session-utils.test.ts -t "session rows expose estimated context budget status" --reporter=verbose`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server.sessions.reset-models.test.ts -t "sessions.reset clears stale estimated context budget status" --reporter=verbose`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Sessions whose model/provider path cannot return fresh context usage can expose OpenClaw's own pre-prompt estimate instead of showing only unknown/zero context usage. Runtime/session lifecycle boundaries now clear the persisted estimate with the rest of stale token/model state so a later session or model cannot show an old budget snapshot.

Real environment tested: WSL2 Ubuntu 24.04 durable PR worktree on refreshed PR head `e1aa1dcd6fb3bebe4b13f206fb6fefff98b397ba`, rebased onto current `origin/main`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: the verification commands listed above, plus a production status-renderer probe using `node --import tsx --input-type=module` against `src/status/status-message.ts` with a persisted stale `totalTokensFresh: false` session entry and current `contextBudgetStatus`.

Evidence after fix: The production status-renderer probe printed `Context: ~640k/1.0m (64% est)`, proving the user-visible `/status` renderer uses the estimate when fresh usage is unavailable. Focused tests proved current estimates persist and render, fresh `totalTokens` still win, gateway rows expose the field, and stale estimates are cleared on run refresh, model override, gateway/manual compaction, native CLI compaction, reply-run reset, `sessions.reset`, and `/new` rotation. Final Codex autoreview returned `autoreview clean: no accepted/actionable findings reported`.

Observed result after fix: `contextBudgetStatus` remains a path-free reporting object for current session estimates, and reset/refresh boundaries clear stale snapshots before `/status` or gateway rows can expose them.

What was not tested: Broad `pnpm check:changed`, full Vitest suite, and a live provider-backed `/status` run were not rerun locally.

target.title: fix(auto-reply): fast path text control commands
target.body:
## Summary
- Add a strict fast path for whole-message text control commands such as `/status`, `/thinking`, `/id`, `/usage`, `/send`, and `/activation`.
- Mark Feishu/Lark whole-message text slash commands as structured text command turns so the core fast path sees the actual channel context shape.
- Reuse the existing status, directive, and local command handlers so these replies can return before workspace/bootstrap or Codex runtime execution.
- Keep risky or prompt-bearing commands on the normal path, including `/new`, `/reset`, `/compact`, `/model`, `/queue`, `/stop`, `/exec`, `/trace`, inline command tokens, and commands with trailing prompt text.

## Root Cause / Impact
Feishu/Lark plain-text slash commands are not native slash commands. They can arrive as authorized text command messages, and Codex/GPT-backed sessions may otherwise route those local controls through the slower normal agent path. That can make `/status` or `/thinking` slow or fail to produce a visible operational reply even though they should be handled locally.

## Review Follow-up
- Addressed ClawSweeper P1 by having Feishu/Lark attach a structured `CommandTurn` with `kind: "text-slash"` for whole-message text slash commands.
- The core fast path now consumes structured text command turns and prefers `CommandTurn.body`, which covers normalized Feishu/Lark command bodies such as mention-stripped group commands.
- Added regression coverage for the actual Feishu-built context shape and for structured text command turns without relying only on legacy `CommandSource: "text"`.

## Related Issues
- Related to #82356, the canonical Feishu/Lark Codex/GPT slash-command `delivered=false` report. This PR addresses the low-risk local control-command subset by returning those replies before runtime execution.
- Related to #84155, which was closed as a duplicate of #82356 and contains newer gpt-5.5 Feishu command no-response logs.
- Adjacent to #78689, but this PR does not claim to fix the broader native Codex Feishu direct-session/message-tool HTTP 400 delivery evidence.
- Adjacent to #79409, but this PR intentionally excludes stateful/destructive/complex commands such as `/compact`, `/new`, and `/stop`, so it does not close that broader authorization/command-handling issue.

## Safety
- Requires the normal text-command enablement check.
- Requires authorized senders before returning local command replies.
- Only handles single-line, whole-message commands from a small whitelist.
- Does not mark inline command tokens such as `please check /status` as text command turns.
- Falls back to the existing reply flow when any prompt text remains.

## Validation
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.auto-reply-reply.config.ts src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply.fast-path.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/feishu/src/bot.test.ts extensions/feishu/src/bot.broadcast.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/auto-reply/command-turn-context.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/source-reply-delivery-mode.test.ts src/channels/inbound-event/context.test.ts`
- Extra WhatsApp regression attempt: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/whatsapp/src/auto-reply/monitor/process-message.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/auto-reply/monitor/inbound-dispatch.test.ts` partially ran; `inbound-dispatch.test.ts` passed, but `process-message.test.ts` could not load locally because `@protobufjs/aspromise` was missing from the interrupted local install.
- `pnpm tsgo:core:test` was attempted but did not complete locally because `pnpm exec`/hook dependency status checks repeatedly stalled on registry downloads for optional platform packages.

## Real behavior proof
Behavior or issue addressed: Feishu/Lark whole-message text control commands such as `/status` should return a local operational reply instead of being routed through the slow Codex/GPT agent path and completing with no visible reply.

Real environment tested: Local macOS OpenClaw gateway started from this PR source at `c81dbf2f13c4`, OpenClaw `2026.5.22`, with the Feishu plugin loaded from `/Users/alex/Code/openclaw/extensions/feishu/index.ts`. The runtime used a real Feishu `feishu2` WebSocket account and a real p2p Feishu DM. Secrets and user/chat IDs are redacted below.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Started a Feishu-only proof gateway from the PR source:

```sh
NODE_DISABLE_COMPILE_CACHE=1 \
OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-pr85616-feishu2-minimal.json \
OPENCLAW_BUNDLED_PLUGINS_DIR=/Users/alex/Code/openclaw/extensions \
OPENCLAW_TEST_TRUST_BUNDLED_PLUGINS_DIR=1 \
OPENCLAW_DISABLE_PERSISTED_PLUGIN_REGISTRY=1 \
OPENCLAW_SKIP_STARTUP_MODEL_PREWARM=1 \
OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_STARTUP_TRACE=1 \
node --import tsx src/entry.ts gateway run --bind loopback --port 18789 --force --ws-log compact --verbose
```

Then sent `/status` from the real Feishu `feishu2` DM.

Evidence after fix: Redacted runtime log excerpt from `/tmp/openclaw/openclaw-2026-05-23.log`:

```text
2026-05-23T21:49:35.639+08:00 [gateway] http server listening (1 plugin: feishu; 29.9s)
2026-05-23T21:49:39.831+08:00 [feishu] starting feishu[feishu2] (mode: websocket)
2026-05-23T21:49:56.165+08:00 [feishu] feishu[feishu2]: WebSocket client started
2026-05-23T21:49:58.334+08:00 [info]: [ '[ws]', 'ws client ready' ]
2026-05-23T21:52:22.137+08:00 [feishu] feishu[feishu2]: received message from <redacted-open-id> in <redacted-chat-id> (p2p)
2026-05-23T21:52:22.150+08:00 [feishu] feishu[feishu2]: Feishu[feishu2] DM from <redacted-open-id>: /status
2026-05-23T21:52:25.438+08:00 [feishu] feishu[feishu2]: dispatch complete (queuedFinal=true, replies=1)
```

For comparison, before this patch on installed OpenClaw `2026.5.20`, the same real Feishu2 `/status` path reached dispatch but produced no visible reply:

```text
2026-05-23T19:45:43.331+08:00 [feishu] feishu[feishu2]: Feishu[feishu2] DM from <redacted-open-id>: /status
2026-05-23T19:45:44.119+08:00 [feishu] feishu[feishu2]: dispatch complete (queuedFinal=false, replies=0)
```

Observed result after fix: The real Feishu2 `/status` DM now exits through the local control-command fast path and produces one queued final reply (`queuedFinal=true, replies=1`) instead of the previous no-reply completion (`queuedFinal=false, replies=0`).

What was not tested: This runtime proof covers the safe local text-control subset via Feishu p2p DM. It does not claim coverage for destructive/stateful commands (`/new`, `/reset`, `/compact`, `/stop`), prompt-bearing slash commands, native slash-command payloads, or group mention routing.

target.title: Preserve Codex and Telegram context evidence
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Telegram prompt context cached inbound messages but not OpenClaw's own outbound Telegram replies, so a later context window could include Keshav's 01:37 request while omitting the adjacent 01:39 assistant answer.
- Problem: normal streamed Telegram assistant finals are finalized through the draft-stream/edit path, so caching only `sendMessageTelegram` sends still missed the central streamed-final path.
- Problem: Codex per-turn context-engine projections were flattening prior tool calls/results to placeholders, hiding evidence needed to answer follow-up debugging questions.
- Solution: cache both successful Telegram outbound sends and preview-finalized streamed finals into the Telegram message-context cache, and preserve sanitized tool evidence in active context-engine projections.
- Scope boundary: this does not make every old transcript globally searchable; it fixes the observed Telegram outbound-context gap and selected-context payload elision only.

## Motivation

Keshav pointed out that a later Codex turn did not show the earlier timeout-halving work or the 01:39 final answer evidence. Rechecking the actual 10:11 session showed no user `/new` or `/reset`: the Telegram selected context included the 01:37 user request but omitted OpenClaw's own 01:39 Telegram reply. A separate later prompt also showed `tool call: bash [input omitted]` and `tool result: ... [content omitted]`.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [x] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Related #85219
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Telegram outbound replies now survive into later Telegram prompt-context windows; Codex context-engine per-turn projections no longer reduce prior tool work to empty placeholders.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on this host, branch `fix/codex-context-tool-payloads`, head commit `8e3ea2be9e3462d3d60da14cf2290ccc796e2dc7`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran targeted Vitest coverage from the repo root. Also checked the production session logs that motivated the fix to confirm the before-state: 10:11 selected Telegram context contained Keshav's 01:37 request but omitted Kelaw's 01:39 reply; 10:18 Codex context contained only `[input omitted]` / `[content omitted]` tool placeholders.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): Candidate runtime proof attached in [comment 4519833550](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/85231#issuecomment-4519833550). The proof harness was temporary and removed after the run, leaving the PR diff unchanged.

  Candidate proof command:

```sh
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run test/manual/pr-85231-candidate-proof.test.ts --reporter verbose
```

  Candidate proof result:

```text
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests       1 passed (1)
```

  Existing targeted regression run from the patched checkout:

```text
Test Files 4 passed (4)
Tests 218 passed (218)
```

- Observed result after fix: The candidate runtime proof invokes the production Telegram outbound-cache helper, reloads the persisted Telegram message cache, and shows the later selected prompt context includes the assistant outbound reply body. It also invokes the Codex context projection with `toolPayloadMode: "preserve"` and shows useful result text like `timeoutSeconds=7200` is retained while the raw shell command and fake API key are absent. The targeted regressions additionally cover normal sends, streamed-final preview finalization, and the run-attempt projection callsite.
- What was not tested: A full deployed Telegram-to-Codex production turn was not exercised. The current Mantis live Telegram catalog does not appear to include a scenario for internal prompt-context retention, so running a generic status/command scenario would not prove this PR's behavior. The attached candidate proof is the smallest available behavior proof without deploying this branch.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): The captured 10:11 local Codex session prompt selected Keshav's 01:37 “halve those” message but not OpenClaw's 01:39 final answer. The captured 10:18 local Codex session prompt showed `tool call: bash [input omitted]` and `tool result: call_... [content omitted]`.

- Production Telegram media proof: GitHub-hosted screenshot of the reported 01:37 -> 01:39 -> 10:11 Telegram thread sequence.

  ![Telegram transcript showing the timeout-halving request, Kelaw confirmation, and later follow-up](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/keshavbotagent/openclaw/proof-pr-85231-telegram-screenshot/proof/pr-85231/telegram-context-proof.jpg)

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: Telegram only recorded inbound messages in `message-cache.ts`; successful outbound sends were recorded for bot ownership/reactions but not for future prompt-context selection.
- Root cause: the first Telegram fix covered `sendMessageTelegram` sends but not preview-finalized streamed finals, which are delivered through draft-stream plus edit-message finalization.
- Root cause: `run-attempt.ts` passed `toolPayloadMode: contextEngineProjection ? "preserve" : "elide"`, so active per-turn context-engine projections used elision even when the projection engine had safe redaction support.
- Missing detection / guardrail: No regression covered Telegram outbound replies being present in later selected conversation context, streamed finals being written to the same cache, or active per-turn context-engine projections preserving redacted tool-result evidence.

## Prompt Boundary Review

ClawSweeper flagged the prompt-boundary expansion in `run-attempt.ts`. I reviewed the projection path and kept the current implementation because the fix needs prior tool-result evidence, not only tool metadata.

Boundary details:

- Tool-call inputs are not preserved as values; only the input shape and primitive types are rendered, so shell commands such as `cat .env` are omitted.
- Tool-result values are passed through `redactToolPayloadText` plus `redactSensitiveFieldValue`, which forces tool-mode redaction and applies built-in/user `logging.redactPatterns`.
- Rendered context is still selected by the context engine and capped by the projection budget; this PR does not add global transcript search.
- The remaining risk is intentional: non-secret private text in selected tool output may become model context. Narrowing that away would also remove the concrete evidence needed to avoid repeated work.

If maintainers do not want that boundary, the smallest alternative is to split this PR and merge only the Telegram outbound-cache fix while leaving Codex tool-result evidence elided.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `extensions/telegram/src/send.test.ts`, `extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts`, `extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.context-engine.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: successful Telegram sends and preview-finalized streamed finals are recorded into the persisted prompt-context cache; active context-engine projection preserves useful tool-result evidence while redacting raw command/secret values.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: it covers both Telegram delivery paths that can produce visible assistant replies and the Codex prompt sent to `turn/start`.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): `context-engine-projection.test.ts` covers projection behavior directly; this PR adds callsite and Telegram outbound-cache coverage.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Future Telegram turns should be able to see OpenClaw's own recent Telegram replies in selected chat context, including streamed assistant finals, so a follow-up should not see only the user's earlier request without the bot answer. Codex should also have enough redacted prior tool-result context to avoid repeating completed work when that evidence exists in selected context.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run test/manual/pr-85231-candidate-proof.test.ts --reporter verbose` (temporary candidate proof harness, removed after run)
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/telegram/src/send.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/message-cache.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.context-engine.test.ts`
- `git diff --check origin/main..HEAD`
- `pnpm check:test-types`

target.title: feat: PluginStatusProvider plugin API for TUI footer status (resolves #64086)
target.body:
Resolves #64086. Coordinates with #53774 (broader footer-customization tracker).

## What this PR does

Adds a typed `PluginStatusProvider` interface and `registerStatusProvider` method to `OpenClawPluginApi`, following the same registration shape as `registerTool` / `registerHook`. Providers are stored in the plugin registry under a new `statusProviders` field.

```ts
export type PluginStatusProvider = {
  /** A unique identifier for this provider. */
  id: string;
  /** Function returning the status string, or null/undefined to hide it. */
  getStatus: () => string | null | undefined;
};
```

Plugin author usage:

```ts
api.registerStatusProvider({
  id: "contextclaw.savings",
  getStatus: () => `cc: -${savedPercent}%`,
});
```

## Scope (deliberately small)

Per @clawsweeper's review on #64127 — *"narrow core/plugin-API design for TUI status contributions: define a typed `PluginStatusProvider`, expose `registerStatusProvider` through `OpenClawPluginApi`, store providers in the plugin registry"* — this PR lands exactly that surface and nothing else.

**Included:**
- `PluginStatusProvider` type + `registerStatusProvider` API entry on `OpenClawPluginApi`
- Plugin registry storage (`statusProviders: PluginStatusProviderRegistration[]`)
- Plumbing through `api-builder.ts`, registry, plugin-entry SDK
- Test helper updates so existing fixtures keep compiling

**Explicitly NOT included (and why):**
- **TUI render-path wire-up** — leaving this for a small follow-up PR so this API surface can stabilize and the maintainer team can shape the rendering UX (placement, ordering, theme-dim, truncation rules, etc.) on its own merits. Happy to send the follow-up as soon as this lands, or roll it in here if reviewers prefer one PR.
- **Cost-display footer drive-by** — was bundled into #64127, removed there in the split, and not added here. Can be revisited under #53774's broader template direction once that scope is settled.

## Plugin-author motivation

I'm shipping [ContextClaw](https://github.com/dodge1218/contextclaw) (a context-engine plugin) which currently has to monkeypatch the bundled TUI to surface live stats — that breaks on every OpenClaw release. A first-class `registerStatusProvider` seam gives plugins a supported path. Real demand, not speculative.

## Coordination with #53774

#53774 is the broader tracker for custom footer fields (cost / balance / rate-limit status, plus a footer template). This PR is **the plugin-API piece** of that vision — it's the seam plugins (and a future `tui.footerTemplate` config feature) can read from. Doesn't preempt #53774's template decisions.

## Files (11, +37 / -0)

- `src/plugins/types.ts` — `PluginStatusProvider` type + `registerStatusProvider` field on `OpenClawPluginApi`
- `src/plugins/registry-types.ts` — `PluginStatusProviderRegistration`, `statusProviders` field on `PluginRegistry`
- `src/plugins/registry.ts` — registry plumbing (`registerStatusProvider` handler)
- `src/plugins/registry-empty.ts` — empty registry init
- `src/plugins/api-builder.ts` — wiring through API builder
- `src/plugin-sdk/plugin-entry.ts` — SDK exposure
- 4 test helpers updated to include the new field
- `src/plugins/status.test-helpers.ts` — minimal helper used by future render-path tests

## Open questions for reviewers

- **Naming**: `getStatus` vs `render` vs `getText` — happy to follow your preference.
- **Return shape**: currently `string | null | undefined`. Should it support `{ text, color }` upfront for theming, or keep it minimal and add color later?
- **Multiple providers per plugin**: registry holds an array, so a single plugin can register multiple providers. Worth documenting / restricting?

Open to design feedback before this lands. Cheers.

commands.review_summary:
This PR hardens Package Acceptance `source=url` downloads, adds policy-backed `source=trusted-url` private mirror support, updates workflow/docs inputs, and adds resolver regression tests.

Reproducibility: yes. Current main's downloader only checks `https:` before fetching `source=url`, so unsafe target acceptance is clear from source inspection even though I did not run a live network repro in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: Manual review is needed because the remaining blockers are release/security ownership decisions and current-head validation, not a narrow automated repair.

target.title: fix: harden package URL downloads
target.body:
## Summary

- Harden `source=url` package candidate downloads so public URL inputs are rejected before fetching when they point at unsafe targets.
- Keep `source=url` as the strict public path: HTTPS only, no URL credentials, default HTTPS port only, private/internal/special-use hostname blocking, DNS/IP blocking, redirect revalidation, timeout and max-size bounds, and atomic `target.tmp` writes.
- Treat DNS as untrusted input. Each URL and redirect is validated, resolved, checked for RFC1918 and broader special-use address space, then fetched through a pinned lookup so resolution cannot rebind between validation and connection.
- Add explicit enterprise/private mirror support through `source=trusted-url` plus `trusted_source_id`, backed by repo-controlled `.github/package-trusted-sources.json` policy instead of an input-level private-network bypass.
- Keep the resolver compatible with the Package Acceptance no-dependency path by using Node built-ins for the real HTTPS download path instead of a top-level `undici` import.
- Fix dotted IPv4-mapped and IPv4-compatible IPv6 parsing so legal text forms such as dotted IPv4 inside IPv6 are normalized into hextets before embedded IPv4 policy checks run.
- Add regression coverage for encoded and dotted IPv6 transition forms that embed private or loopback IPv4 targets.
- Sync the release overview source list so the Package Acceptance overview includes `source=trusted-url` alongside `source=npm`, `source=ref`, `source=url`, and `source=artifact`.

## Security model

The public `source=url` path validates the URL, resolves all A/AAAA answers, rejects blocked address space, pins the HTTPS connection to the validated address set, relies on normal TLS hostname validation, and then verifies the package SHA-256.

The trusted `source=trusted-url` path only permits private resolution when the named trusted-source policy explicitly allows it. DNSSEC is intentionally out of scope. DNSSEC can authenticate DNS data, but it does not determine whether the resolved address is safe for CI to contact.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: `scripts/resolve-openclaw-package-candidate.mjs --source url` previously accepted HTTPS package URLs without the current strict public URL resolution boundary. The patch keeps public `source=url` locked down, adds `source=trusted-url` for maintainer-owned private mirrors, pins the HTTPS connection to validated DNS answers, and fixes dotted IPv4-mapped / IPv4-compatible IPv6 text parsing so unsafe loopback or private targets cannot pass through a legal IPv6 representation.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout at `/home/rev/projects/openclaw` on branch `fix/package-url-download-hardening-clean`, current PR head `93dbbc93251cce9a93bdbcb9f2f5f86e6536f0c1`, running the real Package Acceptance resolver entrypoint with Node and the repo tooling environment. The redacted Package Acceptance-style resolver proof used the same resolver entrypoint, source modes, tarball output path, metadata output path, SHA-256 verification step, trusted-source policy shape, and no-dependency resolver path used by Package Acceptance. Private mirror hostnames, bearer tokens, exact private addresses, package versions, and digest values are redacted.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

    node --check scripts/resolve-openclaw-package-candidate.mjs

    OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run \
      --config test/vitest/vitest.tooling.config.ts \
      test/scripts/resolve-openclaw-package-candidate.test.ts \
      test/scripts/resolve-openclaw-package-candidate-ip-bypass.test.ts \
      test/scripts/package-acceptance-workflow.test.ts

    # Redacted Package Acceptance-style public unsafe URL rejection run
    node scripts/resolve-openclaw-package-candidate.mjs \
      --source url \
      --package-url https://packages.example/[REDACTED]/openclaw-current.tgz \
      --package-sha256 [REDACTED_SHA256] \
      --output-dir .artifacts/docker-e2e-package \
      --metadata .artifacts/docker-e2e-package/package-candidate.json

    # Redacted Package Acceptance-style trusted private mirror success run
    OPENCLAW_TRUSTED_PACKAGE_TOKEN=[REDACTED] \
    node scripts/resolve-openclaw-package-candidate.mjs \
      --source trusted-url \
      --trusted-source-id enterprise-artifactory \
      --package-url https://[REDACTED_PRIVATE_MIRROR]/artifactory/openclaw/openclaw-current.tgz \
      --package-sha256 [REDACTED_SHA256] \
      --output-dir .artifacts/docker-e2e-package \
      --metadata .artifacts/docker-e2e-package/package-candidate.json

Evidence after fix: Copied terminal output and redacted runtime log output from the after-fix resolver/package-acceptance run.

    RUN  v4.1.7 /home/rev/projects/openclaw

     ✓  tooling  test/scripts/package-acceptance-workflow.test.ts (24 tests) 374ms
     ✓  tooling  test/scripts/resolve-openclaw-package-candidate.test.ts (13 tests) 63ms
     ✓  tooling  test/scripts/resolve-openclaw-package-candidate-ip-bypass.test.ts (14 tests) 7ms

     Test Files  3 passed (3)
          Tests  51 passed (51)
       Start at  00:21:44
       Duration  757ms (transform 139ms, setup 141ms, import 42ms, tests 445ms, environment 0ms)

    package_url source: url
    validating package_url: https://packages.example/[REDACTED]/openclaw-current.tgz
    resolving package_url host: packages.example
    resolved addresses: [REDACTED_SPECIAL_USE_ADDRESS]
    fetch attempted: false
    result: rejected before network fetch
    error: Blocked: package_url resolves to private/internal/special-use IP address: [REDACTED_SPECIAL_USE_ADDRESS]

    package_url source: trusted-url
    trusted_source_id: enterprise-artifactory
    trusted source policy: .github/package-trusted-sources.json
    validating host, port, path prefix, redirect hosts, and auth policy
    private resolution allowed by named policy: true
    resolving package_url host: [REDACTED_PRIVATE_MIRROR]
    resolved addresses: [REDACTED_PRIVATE_ADDRESS]
    connection: using pinned lookup result
    download target: .artifacts/docker-e2e-package/openclaw-current.tgz.tmp
    final target: .artifacts/docker-e2e-package/openclaw-current.tgz
    sha256 verified: [REDACTED_SHA256]
    metadata written: .artifacts/docker-e2e-package/package-candidate.json
    result: success

    {
      "name": "openclaw",
      "source": "trusted-url",
      "trustedSourceId": "enterprise-artifactory",
      "packageTrustedReason": "trusted-url-policy:enterprise-artifactory",
      "tarball": ".artifacts/docker-e2e-package/openclaw-current.tgz",
      "sha256": "[REDACTED_SHA256]",
      "version": "[REDACTED_VERSION]"
    }

Observed result after fix: The resolver parsed successfully, the focused resolver/package-acceptance tooling checks passed, and the new IPv6 bypass regression file passed. Public `source=url` rejected unsafe DNS/IP resolution before fetch. Trusted private mirror access succeeded only through named repo-controlled `source=trusted-url` policy, pinned the connection to the validated address set, wrote the expected package candidate artifact path, wrote package metadata, and verified the downloaded tarball SHA-256 before Package Acceptance continued.

What was not tested: A live private Artifactory, Nexus, or S3 mirror using production credentials was not exercised in this public PR proof. Private hostnames, private addresses, digest values, package versions, and bearer token material are intentionally redacted. DNSSEC validation is intentionally out of scope because DNSSEC authenticates DNS data but does not decide whether the resolved address is safe for CI to contact.

## Current-head proof

Current branch head: `93dbbc93251cce9a93bdbcb9f2f5f86e6536f0c1`.

- No-dependency resolver proof: `scripts/resolve-openclaw-package-candidate.mjs` uses Node built-ins for the real download path and no longer has a top-level `import { Agent } from "undici"`.
- Pinned connection proof: the real HTTPS download path resolves the URL host, validates every returned address, then passes `createPinnedLookup(parsed.hostname, addresses)` into `httpsRequest(...)`.
- RFC1918 / special-use proof: public `source=url` resolution is rejected before fetch when any resolved A/AAAA result lands in private/internal/special-use address space. The trusted path only allows private resolution when the named repo-controlled trusted-source policy has `allowPrivateNetwork: true`.
- Dotted IPv4-in-IPv6 parser proof: dotted IPv4 text embedded in IPv4-mapped or IPv4-compatible IPv6 is converted into IPv6 hextets before embedded IPv4 policy classification runs.
- Encoded IPv6 bypass proof: `test/scripts/resolve-openclaw-package-candidate-ip-bypass.test.ts` adds regression coverage for IPv4-mapped, IPv4-compatible, NAT64, 6to4, Teredo, and ISATAP forms that embed private or loopback IPv4 targets.
- Docs proof: `docs/reference/RELEASING.md` now lists `source=trusted-url` as a Package Acceptance source alongside `source=npm`, `source=ref`, `source=url`, and `source=artifact`, while preserving the public references and related sections.
- The bypass tests use an `unexpectedFetch()` helper that throws if the resolver reaches the fetch step, so passing those tests proves rejection happens before any network fetch is attempted.

## Test Plan

- [x] `node --check scripts/resolve-openclaw-package-candidate.mjs`
- [x] `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.tooling.config.ts test/scripts/resolve-openclaw-package-candidate.test.ts test/scripts/resolve-openclaw-package-candidate-ip-bypass.test.ts test/scripts/package-acceptance-workflow.test.ts`
- [x] `pnpm lint`
- [x] `pnpm check:changed`
- [x] Direct runtime proof importing the production `downloadUrl` and `loadTrustedPackageSource` helpers, covering public blocking, trusted private mirror download, and redirect-outside-policy blocking.
- [x] Updated `docs/reference/RELEASING.md` release overview source list to include `source=trusted-url`.
- [x] CI green on current head `93dbbc93251cce9a93bdbcb9f2f5f86e6536f0c1`: CI, CodeQL, OpenGrep, Workflow Sanity, Blacksmith Testbox, and Blacksmith Build Artifacts Testbox.

target.title: fix(tts): defer text settlement for final-mode TTS to eliminate churn (#83511)
target.body:
## Summary

When `messages.tts.mode = "final"`, `finalizeAcpTurnOutput()` calls `settleVisibleText()` before TTS synthesis. This flushes the accumulated text to the channel, then the voice note arrives as a separate supplement — causing delete-and-resend churn on Telegram and other surfaces.

This PR defers `settleVisibleText()` until after the TTS outcome is known, and includes the visible text in the TTS payload so Telegram can deliver it as a voice-note caption.

## Root cause

```
Current main flow:
  1. settleVisibleText()     → text message sent to Telegram
  2. maybeApplyTtsToPayload  → voice note synthesized
  3. deliver(voice + supplement) → voice note sent separately
  Result: user sees text, then voice note, then text deleted = churn

Fixed flow:
  1. Skip settleVisibleText()
  2. maybeApplyTtsToPayload  → voice note synthesized
  3. deliver(voice + text as caption) → one combined message
  4. (If TTS fails → settleVisibleText() fallback)
  Result: user sees one voice note with caption = no churn
```

## Changes

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `dispatch-acp.ts` | Defer `settleVisibleText()` when `ttsMode === "final"` + include text in TTS payload + fallback on failure |
| `dispatch-acp.test.ts` | 4 new tests: deferred settlement for Telegram, eager settlement for non-caption channels, fallback on failure, immediate settlement for non-final |

## Tests

```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.test.ts
 ✓ auto-reply (54 tests) 30ms
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  54 passed (54)
```

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: `settleVisibleText()` called before TTS synthesis in `finalizeAcpTurnOutput()`, causing text-before-audio churn on Telegram.
- **Real environment tested**: Local patched OpenClaw checkout at `6de206309d`, macOS arm64, Node.js `v24.10.0`.
- **Failing proof before fix**: Applied PR test file (`dispatch-acp.test.ts` with 54 tests) to unpatched `upstream/main` (`a002c416c7`) source. 1 test fails:

```
 ❯ auto-reply dispatch-acp.test.ts (54 tests | 1 failed) 47ms

 FAIL  auto-reply > tryDispatchAcpReply > does not call settleVisibleText before TTS synthesis when mode is final
 AssertionError: expected true to be false // Object.is equality

 - Expected
 + Received

 - false
 + true

 ❯ dispatch-acp.test.ts:1928:35

 Test Files  1 failed (1)
      Tests  1 failed | 53 passed (54)
```

The failing test proves that without this patch, `settleVisibleText()` is called **before** TTS synthesis (`settleCalledBeforeTts === true`), confirming the churn bug.

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

```
git checkout origin/fix/tts-final-text-before-audio-83511
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.test.ts
```

- **Evidence after fix**:

```
 ✓ auto-reply dispatch-acp.test.ts (54 tests) 30ms
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  54 passed (54)
```

- **Observed result after fix**: All 54 tests pass including the 4 new tests covering: deferred settlement for Telegram (caption-capable channel), eager settlement for non-caption channels (Discord), text fallback when TTS synthesis fails, and immediate settlement when TTS mode is not "final".
- **What was not tested**: Live Telegram TTS session (requires TTS provider credentials and a live conversation). The settlement deferral logic is validated by 54 unit tests covering the final-mode, fallback, non-final, and channel-gating paths.

Closes #83511

target.title: infra: globalize unhandled rejection state (AI-assisted)
target.body:
## What
- Make installUnhandledRejectionHandler() idempotent so repeated calls do not attach multiple process.on("unhandledRejection", …) listeners.
- Move the unhandled rejection install flag and handler registry into a shared globalThis state keyed by Symbol.for("openclaw.unhandled-rejections.state"), and route registerUnhandledRejectionHandler / isUnhandledRejectionHandled through that shared state.

## Why
- Prevent double-installing unhandled rejection listeners, which can otherwise cause duplicated logs and confusing “why did it exit twice?” behavior if the handler is wired up more than once (tests, embedding, future refactors).
- Centralize unhandled rejection state so different entrypoints share a single source of truth, keeping behavior consistent while remaining a behavior-preserving change in the common case.
​
## AI Assistance
- AI-assisted: Codex
- Testing degree: fully tested
- Screenshots: N/A (non-UI change)

## Validation
- `pnpm build`
- `pnpm check`
- `pnpm test`
- `codex review --base origin/main` (no actionable findings)

## Prompts / Session Context
- Prompt summary: "Use above contribution guidelines as reference and create a PR."
- Work done: analyzed the existing diff, validated gates, and prepared this PR.

## Understanding Confirmation
- This change only refactors unhandled-rejection state ownership and access.
- The existing fatal/transient error classification and process-level listener behavior remain the same.

target.title: Sandbox: validate research-script imports before exec
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: sandboxed `exec` already had shell-bleed preflight, but direct Python/Node script runs could still import unsafe process/network/runtime modules once an operator allowed an interpreter.
- Why it matters: the sandbox should fail fast on obviously unsafe generated research code instead of letting risky imports reach execution time.
- What changed: added deterministic `python-research` and `node-research` import templates, wired them into sandbox-only exec preflight, and documented the behavior.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): host exec behavior, allowlist policy semantics, and non-script/complex shell command parsing.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor
- [x] Docs
- [x] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- Sandboxed `exec` now rejects direct Python/Node research scripts that import blocked runtime/process/network modules or non-allowlisted third-party dependencies.
- Host exec keeps the existing shell-bleed preflight and does not enforce the new research import template.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation:
  - Sandboxed direct script exec is now narrower: Python/Node scripts must stay inside deterministic import allowlists and fail before launch on blocked/dynamic imports.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS
- Runtime/container: Node 22 + pnpm workspace
- Model/provider: n/a
- Integration/channel (if any): n/a
- Relevant config (redacted): sandboxed exec preflight with direct `python*.py` / `node *.js|*.mjs|*.cjs`

### Steps

1. Create a sandboxed script that imports `subprocess` or `node:child_process`.
2. Run it through direct sandboxed `exec` (`python bad.py` or `node bad.js`).
3. Repeat the same Node script through host exec.

### Expected

- Sandboxed direct script exec fails with a clear `exec preflight: sandbox template ... blocks import ...` error.
- Host exec preserves prior behavior.

### Actual

- Matches expected with focused regressions and full suite green locally.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios:
  - `pnpm exec vitest run src/agents/sandbox-execution-templates.test.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec.script-preflight.test.ts`
  - `pnpm build`
  - `pnpm tsgo`
  - `pnpm check`
  - `OPENCLAW_TEST_PROFILE=low OPENCLAW_TEST_SERIAL_GATEWAY=1 pnpm test`
- Edge cases checked:
  - quoted script paths still skip the fast parser
  - sandbox-only enforcement vs host exec
  - local relative/workspace imports remain allowed
  - dynamic `require()` is blocked
- What you did **not** verify:
  - custom/operator-configurable template selection (not added in this PR)

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps:

## Failure Recovery (if this breaks)

- How to disable/revert this change quickly:
  - Revert this commit, or temporarily avoid sandboxed direct Python/Node script exec while investigating.
- Files/config to restore:
  - `src/agents/bash-tools.exec.ts`
  - `src/agents/sandbox-execution-templates.ts`
  - `docs/gateway/sandboxing.md`
- Known bad symptoms reviewers should watch for:
  - legitimate local helper imports rejected in sandboxed script exec
  - false positives on simple direct script commands that previously passed shell-bleed preflight

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk:
  - Regex-based import parsing can miss exotic syntax or reject obscure-but-valid import shapes.
  - Mitigation:
    - kept scope to direct script commands, added focused regressions for local imports/dynamic imports/host-vs-sandbox behavior, and left complex commands on the existing best-effort parser path.

target.title: Doctor: normalize repair checks inside runner
target.body:
## Summary

This PR narrows the first doctor repair migration step to the core doctor runner.

- Adds an internal normalized health-check shape so the repair runner can execute either the existing split `detect()` / `repair()` checks or an internal single-flow `run(ctx)` check.
- Keeps the public plugin SDK health contract unchanged: plugin authors still see `detect()` plus optional `repair()`, and no plugin SDK baseline is regenerated.
- Preserves the old split-check repair sequencing so detected findings are still reported if `repair()` throws.
- Threads dry-run, diff, repair effects, warnings, config updates, and post-repair validation through the normalized runner path.
- Adds focused lint and repair-flow tests covering run-mode checks, split-check adaptation, dry-run/diff preview behavior, repair status mapping, repair failure reporting, and validation after successful repair.

The goal is to make the runner easier to migrate safely without asking every existing doctor check author to split one boolean repair flow immediately. Existing split checks still work through the adapter; internal checks can move to one flow when that is clearer.

## Verification

Local/WSL proof:

- `git diff --check`
- `OPENCLAW_OXLINT_SKIP_PREPARE=1 node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json src/flows/doctor-lint-flow.test.ts src/flows/doctor-repair-flow.test.ts src/flows/doctor-repair-flow.ts src/flows/health-check-adapter.ts src/flows/health-check-runner-types.ts`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-prod-83753.tsbuildinfo`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test-83753-pr1-contract.tsbuildinfo`
- Earlier focused Vitest after adding the split repair-failure regression test: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/flows/doctor-repair-flow.test.ts src/flows/doctor-lint-flow.test.ts` — 2 files / 14 tests passed

Crabbox real CLI proof on current head:

- Provider: AWS Crabbox
- Lease: `cbx_cb0b70795a87`
- Run: `run_85627cfd426c`
- Portal: https://crabbox.openclaw.ai/portal/runs/run_85627cfd426c
- Head: `d139d39e884019ce5de521660ba382a0499a1575`
- Command: disposable `OPENCLAW_HOME` with legacy browser residue, then `doctor --lint --only core/doctor/browser-clawd-profile-residue --json`, `doctor --fix --yes --non-interactive --no-workspace-suggestions`, and the same lint command again.

Crabbox transcript excerpt, with temp home redacted:

```text
HEAD=d139d39e884019ce5de521660ba382a0499a1575
NODE=v24.15.0
PNPM=11.2.2
CONFIG=<temp-home>/.openclaw/openclaw.json
LEGACY_BEFORE=present
== doctor --lint before ==
{"ok":false,"checksRun":1,"checksSkipped":16,"findings":[{"checkId":"core/doctor/browser-clawd-profile-residue","severity":"warning","message":"Legacy managed browser profile residue was found at <temp-home>/.openclaw/browser/clawd.","path":"<temp-home>/.openclaw/browser/clawd","ocPath":"oc://state/browser/clawd","fixHint":"Run `openclaw doctor --fix` to archive the stale clawd profile safely instead of deleting it in place."}]}
== doctor --fix ==
Archived legacy clawd managed browser profile residue.
- legacy profile: <temp-home>/.openclaw/browser/clawd
- canonical profile: <temp-home>/.openclaw/browser/openclaw/user-data
LEGACY_AFTER=absent
== doctor --lint after ==
{"ok":true,"checksRun":1,"checksSkipped":16,"findings":[]}
```

## Real behavior proof

## Description

Surface uniform provider billing data in `openclaw models status --json` with metered provider support (OpenRouter), existing windowed providers (Copilot, Codex) and now have consistent, queryable structure across providers.

## What Changed

### Schema Unification

Both `windowed` (subscription-based) and `metered` (account-balance) providers now have consistent structure in JSON output:

**Windowed** (GitHub Copilot, OpenAI Codex):
```json
{
  "kind": "windowed",
  "displayName": "Copilot",
  "status": "ok",
  "summary": "Premium 5% left · Chat 100% left",
  "windows": [
    { "name": "Premium", "usedPercent": 95.3, "remainingPercent": 4.7, "resetAt": null },
    { "name": "Chat", "usedPercent": 0, "remainingPercent": 100, "resetAt": null }
  ]
}
```

**Metered** (OpenRouter):
```json
{
  "kind": "metered",
  "displayName": "OpenRouter",
  "status": "ok",
  "account": {
    "totalCredits": 185,
    "totalUsage": 166.91,
    "remainingCredits": 18.09,
    "usedPercent": 90.22

}
```

- **Conditional fetches**: All provider usage (windowed + metered) only fetched when `--json` flag used
- **Parallelized requests**: OpenRouter per-key calls run concurrently (5 workers) instead of sequentially
- **Latency**: Eliminated baseline cost for `--plain`/`--check` modes; parallel window reduced N-key fetch from `(N+1)×3.5s` to `~3.5s`

target.title: feat(routing): support per-agent dmScope override
target.body:
## Summary

- Add optional `dmScope` field to `AgentConfig`, allowing each agent to override the global `session.dmScope`
- When set, the per-agent `dmScope` takes precedence over `session.dmScope` during session key resolution
- Falls back to the global `session.dmScope` when no per-agent override is configured

### Use case

When running multiple agents with different isolation needs:
- **Personal agent** (`dmScope: "main"`): all DMs collapse to a single shared session
- **DM agent** (inherits global `per-channel-peer`): each user gets an isolated session
- **Group agent**: unaffected by dmScope (groups always get their own session key)

```jsonc
{
  "session": { "dmScope": "per-channel-peer" },
  "agents": {
    "list": [
      { "id": "main", "dmScope": "main" },
      { "id": "dm" },
      { "id": "group" }
    ]
  }
}
```

### Files changed

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `src/config/types.agents.ts` | Add `dmScope?: DmScope` to `AgentConfig` |
| `src/config/zod-schema.agent-runtime.ts` | Add zod validation for `dmScope` |
| `src/routing/resolve-route.ts` | Look up per-agent `dmScope` in `choose()` |
| `src/config/schema.help.ts` | Add help text |
| `src/config/schema.labels.ts` | Add label |
| `src/routing/resolve-route.test.ts` | Add test for per-agent override |

## Test plan

- [x] `npx vitest run src/routing/resolve-route.test.ts` — all 42 tests pass
- [ ] Manual: configure two agents with different `dmScope` values, verify session keys

target.title: fix(agents): classify compound read-only gateway actions correctly
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Compound gateway action names like `config.schema.lookup` are classified as mutating because `isMutatingToolCall` only checks the full action string against `READ_ONLY_ACTIONS`, and compound names are never in that set.
- Why it matters: Read-only gateway operations produce false "mutating action" classifications, which trigger unnecessary user-facing error notifications on tool failures.
- What changed: Added `lookup` to `READ_ONLY_ACTIONS` and updated the gateway/cron/canvas branch to extract the leaf verb from compound action names before checking the set.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): The `_actions`-suffixed and `nodes` branches are left as-is since they do not use compound naming.

target.title: feat(daemon): auto-detect Node.js heap limit; add gateway.nodeMaxOldSpaceMb config
target.body:
## Problem

Node.js defaults to a ~1.5 GB V8 heap ceiling regardless of available system RAM. On machines with 8–128 GB this leaves most memory unused and causes hard OOM aborts (`CheckIneffectiveMarkCompact` → `FatalProcessOutOfMemory` → SIGABRT) under heavy parallel agent workloads. The issue also affects child Node processes spawned by the gateway, which inherit no heap configuration at all.

## Solution

At gateway install time, automatically compute a sane heap limit (85% of `os.totalmem()`) and inject it into `NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=N` in the service environment. This propagates to child Node processes automatically via environment inheritance.

Users who want a specific value can set `gateway.nodeMaxOldSpaceMb` in their config. The interactive onboarding wizard shows the auto-detected value and lets them override it.

## Changes

- **`src/config/types.gateway.ts`** — adds `nodeMaxOldSpaceMb?: number` to `GatewayConfig`
- **`src/config/zod-schema.ts`** — schema: `z.number().int().min(256).optional()`
- **`src/config/schema.labels.ts`** / **`schema.help.ts`** — label + help text following existing conventions
- **`src/daemon/service-env.ts`** — adds `resolveDefaultNodeMaxOldSpaceMb()` and `resolveNodeOptions()` (both exported for testing); injects `NODE_OPTIONS` in `buildCommonServiceEnvironment`; adds `nodeMaxOldSpaceMb?` param to `buildServiceEnvironment`
- **`src/commands/daemon-install-helpers.ts`** — threads `config?.gateway?.nodeMaxOldSpaceMb` into `buildServiceEnvironment`
- **`src/wizard/onboarding.gateway-config.ts`** — adds heap prompt to non-quickstart flow, showing auto-detected value as hint, blank = auto-detect
- **`src/daemon/service-env.test.ts`** — 10 new tests covering auto-detect math, env override precedence, flag composition, `buildServiceEnvironment` integration

## Behaviour details

| Scenario | Result |
|---|---|
| No config, no `NODE_OPTIONS` | `NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=<85% of RAM>` |
| `gateway.nodeMaxOldSpaceMb: 6144` set | `NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=6144` |
| User already has `--max-old-space-size` in `NODE_OPTIONS` | Left unchanged (no override) |
| User has other flags in `NODE_OPTIONS` | Heap flag appended: `<existing> --max-old-space-size=<N>` |
| Quickstart flow | Auto-detect applied silently, no prompt shown |

## Testing

```
pnpm vitest run src/daemon/service-env.test.ts src/config/config-misc.test.ts src/config/schema.test.ts
```

59 + 32 + 20 = 111 tests, all passing.
