target.title: fix(auth): keep alias-compatible auth-profile overrides instead of clearing them
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem:** A claude-cli session's stored `authProfileOverride` was cleared whenever the profile's raw credential `provider` string was not literally in the accepted-providers list — for example an `anthropic`-provider credential serving a `claude-cli` run. Auto-selection then re-picked a different alias-compatible profile, so the CLI session's auth profile oscillated between `anthropic:claude-cli` and `anthropic:default` across consecutive turns.
- **Solution:** Use the alias-aware compatibility helper (`isStoredCredentialCompatibleWithAuthProvider`) that `session-override.ts` already trusts, so an alias-compatible override is kept rather than cleared.
- **What changed:** the eligibility check in `model-selection.ts` (raw provider-string `includes` → alias-aware compatibility helper).
- **What did NOT change:** the auto-selection ordering, user-sourced overrides, non-claude-cli providers, the `session-override.ts` selection path itself.

## Motivation

Each profile flip mismatched the persisted CLI session binding's `authProfileId`, invalidating the session (`invalidatedReason: "auth-profile"`), forcing a fresh `claude` session and dropping conversation continuity. A self-inflicted, recurring history loss that fired multiple times a day on the affected install.

## Change Type

- [x] Bug fix

## Scope

- [x] Auth (auth-profile selection)

## Linked Issue/PR

Related to claude-cli session invalidation / history loss. Checked: this PR fixes a bug or regression.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed:** auto-selected `authProfileOverride` oscillating between two alias-compatible Anthropic profiles (`anthropic:claude-cli` and `anthropic:default`) across consecutive turns of the same claude-cli session, repeatedly invalidating its persisted CLI session binding with `reason=auth-profile` and dropping conversation history.
- **Real environment tested:** OpenClaw on WSL2 Ubuntu, claude-cli backend, two configured Anthropic auth profiles — `anthropic:claude-cli` (oauth) and `anthropic:default` (token) — and `~/.openclaw/openclaw.json` setting `auth.order.anthropic = ["anthropic:default","anthropic:claude-cli"]`. The two profiles are alias-compatible: both serve the `claude-cli` provider via `isStoredCredentialCompatibleWithAuthProvider`, but only one is literally `"claude-cli"` as a raw credential `provider` string.
- **Before evidence:** the affected install's live state showed the two writers disagreeing on which profile owns the session, captured live and redacted — terminal output below:

```
~/.openclaw/agents/<agent>/agent/auth-state.json   lastGood.anthropic = "anthropic:claude-cli"
~/.openclaw/agents/<agent>/sessions/sessions.json
  agent:<agent>:<session>.authProfileOverride           = "anthropic:default"
  agent:<agent>:<session>.authProfileOverrideSource     = "auto"
  agent:<agent>:<session>.cliSessionBindings.claude-cli.authProfileId = "anthropic:default"
  agent:<agent>:<session>.cliSessionBindings.claude-cli.authEpoch     = "be6a55fc818fb4983484cff8e36d89fae96006f099e9926423bb534b93d705d8"
```

Gateway production logs at the moment the override flipped (redacted runtime logs):

```
[agent/cli-backend] cli session reset: provider=claude-cli reason=auth-profile
[agent/cli-backend] cli exec: provider=claude-cli model=opus useResume=false reuse=invalidated:auth-profile
```

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** with two alias-compatible Anthropic profiles configured under `auth.order.anthropic`, drive consecutive turns on a claude-cli session (any sequence of two or more turns over a few minutes is enough). The previous behaviour clears the override on one turn and re-picks the other; the patched behaviour leaves the override pinned.

- **Evidence after fix:** on the patched-build gateway, the same session's persisted `authProfileOverride` stays pinned across consecutive turns instead of oscillating between the two alias-compatible profiles. Captured live terminal output from the patched run (redacted runtime logs):

```
~/.openclaw/agents/<agent>/sessions/sessions.json
  agent:<agent>:<session>.authProfileOverride       = "anthropic:default"  (turn N)
  agent:<agent>:<session>.authProfileOverride       = "anthropic:default"  (turn N+1)
  agent:<agent>:<session>.authProfileOverride       = "anthropic:default"  (turn N+2)
```

The `model-selection.ts` eligibility check now returns `compatible: true` for the alias-compatible Anthropic credential when the run provider is `claude-cli`, matching the result `session-override.ts` already returned for the same input — so the two writers no longer disagree and the override is no longer cleared and re-picked.

- **Observed result after fix:** on the patched-build gateway, the persisted `authProfileOverride` for the affected session stayed pinned across the run; no `cli session reset: reason=auth-profile` line was emitted for that session in the local gateway's runtime logs for the duration of the observation window; the CLI session binding's `authProfileId` matched the override across consecutive turns; conversation continuity was preserved.
- **What was not tested:** the second rotation source observed on the same install — `resolveSessionAuthProfileOverride` rotating profiles on billing cooldown / compaction — is a separate bug path and is intentionally not addressed by this PR; sessions can still flap under that path until a follow-up lands.

## Root Cause

`model-selection.ts` checked the profile's raw credential `provider` against `acceptedAuthProviders` with a plain string `includes`. For a `claude-cli` run, `acceptedAuthProviders` is `["claude-cli"]`, so an `anthropic`-provider credential was judged ineligible and the persisted override was cleared. `session-override.ts` on the same call path uses the alias-aware helper `isStoredCredentialCompatibleWithAuthProvider`, which correctly accepts the same credential. The two writers disagreed for every alias-compatible profile, producing the oscillation. Missing guardrail: the two call sites must agree on what counts as a compatible profile.

## Regression Test Plan

- `src/auto-reply/reply/model-selection.test.ts` and `src/agents/auth-profiles/session-override.test.ts`: assert that a `claude-cli` selection does not clear an alias-compatible `anthropic:default` override and that the override stays pinned across consecutive runs. Coverage focuses on the eligibility check returning `compatible: true` for the alias-compatible case.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

None directly visible. The fix eliminates spurious CLI session resets and the associated conversation-history loss for users running with two alias-compatible Anthropic profiles in `auth.order.anthropic`.

## Diagram

```text
Before: claude-cli run + anthropic:default override -> raw provider check fails -> override CLEARED -> re-pick -> flap -> session reset
After:  claude-cli run + anthropic:default override -> alias-aware check passes -> override KEPT  -> stable     -> session preserved
```

## Security Impact

- New permissions: No
- New secrets handling: No
- New network calls: No
- New exec surface: No
- New data access: No (uses an existing, already-imported compatibility helper)

## Repro + Verification

- **Environment:** a claude-cli session whose `authProfileOverride` is an alias-compatible non-`claude-cli` profile (for example an `anthropic`-provider token credential alongside the `claude-cli` OAuth profile).
- **Steps:** run consecutive turns on the session (or trigger a `/model` directive turn).
- **Expected:** the override stays pinned across turns.
- **Actual (before):** the override is cleared and re-picked, invalidating the CLI session and dropping conversation history.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing-then-passing regression coverage
- [x] Trace logs / terminal capture (before: persisted-state snapshot + production runtime logs; after: persisted-state snapshot from the patched-build run)

## Human Verification

Verified: the source-level eligibility check now matches the alias-aware helper used by the sibling call site; the persisted-state snapshot on the patched-build gateway shows the `authProfileOverride` pinned across consecutive turns for the affected session; no `cli session reset: reason=auth-profile` line was emitted for that session for the duration of the patched-build observation window. NOT verified: a multi-day soak across many sessions; the separate `resolveSessionAuthProfileOverride` rotation path on billing cooldown / compaction (out of scope for this PR).

## Review Conversations

- [x] Will resolve bot review threads before merge
- [x] Will respond to maintainer review comments

## Compatibility / Migration

Backward compatible. No config or migration. The fix only widens the set of profiles considered compatible to match the sibling call site's existing behaviour.

## Risks and Mitigations

- **Risk:** the check now keeps more overrides — but only those genuinely alias-compatible per the same helper `session-override.ts` already trusts.
  **Mitigation:** consistency between the two call sites is the explicit goal; widening here is bounded by the helper that already passes review on the sibling path.
- **Risk:** a future provider whose alias mapping changes could allow an unexpected override to survive.
  **Mitigation:** any change to alias mapping would land in `isStoredCredentialCompatibleWithAuthProvider` and apply uniformly to both call sites, preserving consistency by construction.

target.title: fix(cron): route topic targets through channel plugins
target.body:
## Summary

- Moves cron announce target parsing for thread/topic ids behind the generic channel plugin parser instead of hard-coding Telegram forum syntax in cron core.
- Keeps Telegram-owned `chatId:topicId` and `chatId:topic:topicId` parsing in the Telegram plugin, with cron consuming only the generic channel parser contract.
- Updates cron delivery docs, changelog, and focused regression coverage for plugin-owned topic parsing.

Thanks @etticat for the original report and PR #49704.

## Verification

- `pnpm test src/infra/outbound/targets.test.ts src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-target.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/targets.test.ts src/cron/isolated-agent.direct-delivery-core-channels.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `pnpm format:docs:check`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local --prompt "Review the refactor that moves cron delivery target topic parsing behind generic channel plugin target parsing. Focus on ownership boundary, Telegram forum topic target correctness, stale thread carry, and delivery regression risk. Do not complain about missing live Telegram proof; this is a local replacement PR prep."`
- `pnpm check:changed` via Blacksmith Testbox `tbx_01ksa97e0fgg7z3m6sq5h6bc99`, GitHub Actions run 26331385142

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: cron announce delivery resolves Telegram/forum topic ids through the owning channel plugin parser instead of cron core before direct delivery.
Real environment tested: local unit/integration tests plus Blacksmith Testbox `tbx_01ksa97e0fgg7z3m6sq5h6bc99` changed gate.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `pnpm test src/infra/outbound/targets.test.ts src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-target.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/targets.test.ts src/cron/isolated-agent.direct-delivery-core-channels.test.ts`; `pnpm check:changed`.
Evidence after fix: focused tests passed 3 Vitest shards in 19.69s after rebase; Testbox changed gate exited 0 in run 26331385142.
Observed result after fix: Telegram `:topic:` and `:topicId` targets resolve to base chat id plus `threadId`; cron core no longer contains Telegram-specific topic stripping.
What was not tested: live Telegram Bot API/forum topic delivery with a real chat was not run in this replacement PR.

Replaces #49704.

commands.author: Kaspre

target.title: fix(gateway): honor restart drain budget for embedded runs
target.body:
Problem

Gateway restarts currently treat active embedded runs differently from active task runs: embedded runs hit a hidden 30s abort grace even when the restart drain budget is configured longer, or explicitly unbounded. That can interrupt legitimate long embedded runs even though operators configured the gateway to wait.

Why this is not superseded by #71465

This is a replacement for #85630, which was closed as duplicate/superseded by #71465. #71465 is related, but it did not remove the embedded-run-specific cap that remains on current `main`.

#71465 established the restart drain path and made the general deferral budget visible, but current `main` still has `RESTART_ACTIVE_EMBEDDED_RUN_ABORT_GRACE_MS = 30_000` and still routes active embedded runs through `resolveActiveRunDrainWaitMs()`. That means embedded runs are still capped at 30 seconds even when the operator-visible restart drain budget is longer or explicitly unbounded. This PR is the focused follow-up for that remaining hidden embedded-run cap.

Change and value

This PR makes active embedded runs use the same restart drain budget as active task runs. It also threads a SIGUSR1 restart intent through the scheduled restart path so a restart that already waited through the pre-restart deferral timeout is treated as forced by the run loop instead of spending the same budget a second time.

Who is affected

Operators using `gateway.reload.deferralTimeoutMs`, restart `waitMs`, config reloads that require a gateway restart, or long-running embedded Pi runs during restart.

Who is not affected

This does not change ordinary stop behavior, restart authorization, restart scheduling permissions, or public configuration shape. It only removes the hidden embedded-run cap so embedded runs follow the existing restart drain budget.

Why now

The documented deferral timeout is the restart budget users can see and configure. Keeping a separate hidden embedded-run cap makes the behavior harder to reason about and can discard work sooner than configured.

Implementation

- Removed the separate `RESTART_ACTIVE_EMBEDDED_RUN_ABORT_GRACE_MS` cap.
- Pass `restartDrainTimeoutMs` directly to `waitForActiveEmbeddedRuns`.
- Added in-memory SIGUSR1 restart intent plumbing for scheduled restarts.
- Mark deferral-timeout restarts as `{ force: true }` so the run loop skips a second active-work drain after the deferral budget has already been exhausted.
- Carry forced restart reasons into main-session restart recovery markers.
- Clear the in-flight SIGUSR1 token when a file intent handles an authorized restart signal.

Maintainer question

This PR treats explicit unbounded restart waits (`waitMs: 0` or `gateway.reload.deferralTimeoutMs: 0`) as unbounded for embedded runs too, matching active task behavior. If maintainers prefer an embedded-run hard watchdog even when the overall restart drain is unbounded, I can revise toward a separate explicit cap instead of a hidden 30s grace.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Active embedded runs now honor the configured restart drain budget instead of a hidden 30s cap, and deferral-timeout SIGUSR1 restarts skip a second drain.
- Real environment tested: Local contributor worktree on replacement head `2c0889f7f1`; the branch was created from `origin/main` `55a0c9b1f4` and GitHub reports the PR mergeable against current base `4ec85762ab`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

  ```sh
  env OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.cli.config.ts src/cli/gateway-cli/run-loop.test.ts --reporter=verbose
  env OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.infra.config.ts src/infra/restart.deferral-timeout.test.ts src/infra/infra-runtime.test.ts --reporter=verbose
  env OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway.config.ts src/gateway/server-reload-handlers.test.ts --reporter=verbose
  pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/infra/restart.ts src/cli/gateway-cli/lifecycle.runtime.ts src/cli/gateway-cli/run-loop.ts src/cli/gateway-cli/run-loop.test.ts src/infra/restart.deferral-timeout.test.ts src/infra/infra-runtime.test.ts src/gateway/server-reload-handlers.ts src/gateway/server-reload-handlers.test.ts
  git diff --check HEAD^..HEAD
  node --import tsx /tmp/openclaw-restart-drain-proof.mjs
  ```
- Evidence after fix: Terminal transcript from `node --import tsx /tmp/openclaw-restart-drain-proof.mjs` exercised real `runGatewayLoop`, `ACTIVE_EMBEDDED_RUNS`, `emitGatewayRestart`, and `deferGatewayRestartUntilIdle` paths and printed this after-fix output:

  ```json
  {
    "exitCode": 0,
    "startCount": 3,
    "waitZero": {
      "drainedWithoutAbort": true,
      "close": {
        "reason": "gateway restarting",
        "restartExpectedMs": 1500,
        "drainTimeoutMs": 15000
      }
    },
    "deferralTimeout": {
      "abortCount": 1,
      "close": {
        "reason": "gateway restarting",
        "restartExpectedMs": 1500,
        "drainTimeoutMs": 0
      }
    },
    "eventKinds": [
      "start",
      "embedded.drained",
      "close",
      "start",
      "embedded.abort",
      "close",
      "start",
      "close",
      "exit"
    ]
  }
  ```

  Targeted exact-head checks also passed: CLI restart loop suite, 35 tests; infra restart suites, 34 tests; gateway reload suite, 11 tests; formatting check for all 8 changed files; `git diff --check HEAD^..HEAD`.
- Observed result after fix: The proof showed an explicit unbounded restart wait draining an active embedded run without aborting it, and a deferral-timeout restart aborting once with `drainTimeoutMs: 0` instead of spending a second active-work drain budget.
- What was not tested: Full remote validation for this replacement head is left to GitHub CI. #85630, which carried the same scoped diff before replacement, completed remote GCP/Crabbox validation, multi-lane review, Claude `--print`, Codex review, and GitHub CI before it was closed as superseded.

Review gates

- Same scoped diff as #85630.
- #85630 completed multi-lane read-only review; accepted findings were fixed or explicitly classified.
- #85630 completed Claude `--print` multi-lane review after increasing timeout to 20 minutes per lane; no blockers remained.
- #85630 completed Codex review with no discrete actionable bugs.
- Replacement head reran targeted local checks and real behavior proof after cherry-picking onto a fresh main worktree.

Changelog

No changelog entry added; leaving release-note/changelog ownership to maintainers.

target.title: fix: use FLAG_TERMINATOR constant in getCommandPathInternal
target.body: Closes #83902 `getCommandPathInternal` was the only function in `argv.ts` using a hardcoded `"--"` instead of the imported `FLAG_TERMINATOR` constant. Replaced the literal with the constant for consistency — if FLAG_TERMINATOR ever changes, command-path resolution will now stay in sync with all other argv helpers. ## Real behavior proof Behavior addressed: getCommandPathInternal used a hardcoded "--" literal that was inconsistent with the FLAG_TERMINATOR constant used elsewhere in argv.ts; unified to the constant. Behavior is preserved — "--" is still the flag terminator at runtime. Real environment tested: local macOS (darwin 25.5.0), Node 24, pnpm 11.2.2, built openclaw CLI from this branch Exact steps or command run after this patch: 1. pnpm install && pnpm build 2. pnpm openclaw status -- --foo --bar 3. (supplemental) pnpm test src/cli/argv.test.ts → 86 tests passed 4. (supplemental) pnpm check:changed → all green Evidence after fix (real CLI output): ``` $ pnpm openclaw status -- --foo --bar $ node scripts/run-node.mjs status -- --foo --bar 🦞 OpenClaw 2026.5.22 (4b4be8a) OpenClaw status ... (full status table rendered; "--" correctly terminated flag parsing, --foo --bar passed as positional args, no parse error) ``` (See attached screenshot below for full terminal capture.) Observed result after fix: openclaw CLI parses command path identically to before. `--` still terminates flag scanning and downstream args are passed through to the command's positional/raw argv. No regression in any of the touched code paths. What was not tested: full e2e suite, non-macOS platforms

target.title: fix(session): prefer real tool result over synthetic error in transcript repair
target.body:
## Summary

Fix transcript repair to prefer real tool results over synthetic "missing tool result" errors during deduplication.

When a channel (e.g. Feishu) has a flush-race condition, the session-tool-result-guard may write a synthetic error result before the real result arrives from the channel. On the next session load, `repairToolUseResultPairing` deduplicates by keeping the first-seen result — which is the synthetic error. The user sees "Something went wrong" even though the tool call actually succeeded.

This PR adds `isSyntheticMissingToolResult()` detection and replaces an existing synthetic result with a later real result during deduplication.

> **Note:** This is a transcript-repair mitigation. The underlying flush-race in the session-tool-result-guard (#84134) is a separate root-cause concern tracked independently.

## Changes

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `session-transcript-repair.ts` | Add `isSyntheticMissingToolResult()` detector; track output positions; replace synthetic with real during dedup |
| `session-transcript-repair.test.ts` | 5 regression tests: synthetic-first, real-first, two-real, two-synthetic, span-level replacement |

## Tests

```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/session-transcript-repair.test.ts
 ✓ unit-fast ../../src/agents/session-transcript-repair.test.ts (40 tests) 5ms
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  40 passed (40)
```

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: When a synthetic "missing tool result" error is persisted before the real result arrives (Feishu channel flush race), the repair pass keeps the synthetic error and the user sees "Something went wrong" even though the tool call succeeded.
- **Real environment tested**: macOS arm64, Node.js `v24.10.0`, running the actual `repairToolUseResultPairing` function from the source tree against a realistic Feishu session scenario.

### Failing proof before fix (main branch)

```
$ npx tsx proof-84419-runner.mts  # on main branch

--- Input: session with synthetic-first, real-second tool result ---
  Messages: 4
  Tool results: 2 (1 synthetic error, 1 real success)
  Scenario: Feishu flush race — synthetic error persisted before real result arrived

--- Output: after repair ---
  Messages: 4
  Tool results: 2
  Kept result isError: true
  Kept result text: "[openclaw] missing tool result in session history; ..."

❌ FAIL: Synthetic error was kept
   → User sees "Something went wrong" even though message was delivered
```

### Passing proof after fix (this PR)

```
$ npx tsx proof-84419-runner.mts  # on fix branch

--- Input: session with synthetic-first, real-second tool result ---
  Messages: 4
  Tool results: 2 (1 synthetic error, 1 real success)

--- Output: after repair ---
  Messages: 3
  Tool results: 1
  Kept result isError: false
  Kept result text: "Message sent successfully to Feishu user feishu_user_123"

✅ PASS: Real result preferred over synthetic error
   → User sees successful delivery, not "Something went wrong"

--- Scenario 2: real-first, synthetic-second ---
  Kept result isError: false
✅ PASS: Real result kept (synthetic duplicate dropped)
```

- **Evidence after fix**: The repair function correctly identifies synthetic "missing tool result" markers and replaces them with real results when both exist for the same tool call ID. Both orderings (synthetic-first and real-first) produce the correct outcome.
- **What was not tested**: Live Feishu channel reproduction (requires a configured Feishu bot with the specific flush-race timing). The underlying flush race in `session-tool-result-guard` (#84134) is not addressed by this PR — it mitigates the downstream effect in the transcript repair pass.

target.title: fix(gateway): run daily session resets on schedule
target.body:
## Summary

- Daily session resets now run from gateway maintenance instead of waiting for the next inbound session use.
- The scheduler scans configured session stores and calls the existing reset service for stale daily sessions after the configured boundary.
- The maintenance timer now reads current runtime config on each sweep, so hot config changes to `session.reset`, `session.resetByType`, `session.resetByChannel`, or store targets are applied without a gateway restart.
- Docs now describe proactive daily rollover and the implicit provider-owned CLI session exemption.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [x] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [x] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [x] CI/CD / infra
- [x] Docs

## Linked Issue/PR

- Fixes #82321
- Related: N/A
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Motivation

A daily reset with `atHour` is a wall-clock policy. Users should not need to send a new message after the reset boundary before stale session context is cleared. The maintenance sweep should also honor the current runtime config after hot reloads, not the startup snapshot.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Daily sessions stale past `atHour` are reset by gateway maintenance without waiting for a new inbound message, while active runs and implicit provider-owned CLI sessions are preserved.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout in a Linux container, on branch `fix-daily-session-reset`, running the patched gateway maintenance timer against an isolated temp `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`. Bundled plugin facades were disabled for the proof process with `OPENCLAW_DISABLE_BUNDLED_PLUGINS=1` so the reset service did not depend on source-checkout plugin artifacts.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - Ran a live Node command that starts `startGatewayMaintenanceTimers` with the patched daily reset scheduler, current-config getter, real `performGatewaySessionReset` path, isolated temp session store, one stale daily session, one stale active-run session, and one implicit provider-owned CLI session.
  - Command shape: `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=<tmp> OPENCLAW_DISABLE_BUNDLED_PLUGINS=1 node --import tsx - <<'TS' ... startGatewayMaintenanceTimers(...) ... TS`
  - Supplemental verification: `./node_modules/.bin/vitest run src/gateway/session-daily-reset-scheduler.test.ts src/gateway/server-maintenance.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway.config.ts`
  - Supplemental type gates: `pnpm check:test-types && pnpm tsgo:prod`
- Evidence after fix:
  ```text
  $ OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=<tmp> OPENCLAW_DISABLE_BUNDLED_PLUGINS=1 node --import tsx - <<'TS'
  # creates isolated agents/main/sessions/sessions.json
  # starts patched gateway maintenance timers
  # waits for daily reset sweep to invoke the real reset service
  TS
  gateway-proof: before {"stale":"old-regular-session","active":"old-active-session","cli":"old-cli-session"}
  [session-daily-reset] daily session reset sweep complete
  gateway-proof: after {"stale":"5f959c12-f0ed-4ed1-99d2-cc158c66ac87","active":"old-active-session","cli":"old-cli-session"}
  gateway-proof: staleReset true
  gateway-proof: activePreserved true
  gateway-proof: implicitCliPreserved true

  $ ./node_modules/.bin/vitest run src/gateway/session-daily-reset-scheduler.test.ts src/gateway/server-maintenance.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway.config.ts
  Test Files  2 passed (2)
  Tests       18 passed (18)

  $ pnpm check:test-types && pnpm tsgo:prod
  exit_code=0
  ```
- Observed result after fix: The running gateway maintenance timer reset the stale daily session through the actual reset service without any inbound message. The active-run session and implicit provider-owned CLI session kept their original session IDs. Focused coverage also verifies fresh sessions are left alone and later sweeps use current runtime config after reset policy changes.
- What was not tested: A multi-hour live gateway wait that naturally crosses the configured reset hour.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

Daily reset freshness was only evaluated through session-use paths. If no message arrived after `atHour`, the stale session could remain active even though the configured reset time had passed. The initial scheduler also captured the startup config, which meant hot reloads could leave the sweep using stale reset policy until restart.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level:
  - [ ] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file:
  - `src/gateway/session-daily-reset-scheduler.test.ts`
  - `src/gateway/server-maintenance.test.ts`
- Locked-in scenarios:
  - A stale daily session resets during a scheduler sweep without a new message.
  - A fresh daily session is left alone.
  - Implicit provider-owned CLI sessions are not cut by the default daily policy.
  - Sessions with active runs are skipped.
  - Scheduler sweeps use current runtime config after a reset-policy change.
- Existing coverage before this patch covered stale detection when called, but not proactive scheduled execution or reload-aware maintenance behavior.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Configured daily session resets now happen on schedule during gateway uptime, even if no message arrives after the reset boundary. The public docs now state that proactive rollover can happen from gateway maintenance and that implicit provider-owned CLI sessions keep continuity unless reset is explicit.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
daily reset boundary
  -> gateway maintenance sweep
  -> read current runtime config
  -> skip active runs and implicit provider-owned CLI sessions
  -> stale daily sessions reset through existing reset service
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- No security-impacting changes identified.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux container
- Runtime/container: Local OpenClaw checkout
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel: N/A
- Relevant config: `session.reset.mode=daily`, `session.reset.atHour=4`

### Steps

1. Create a session store entry with `sessionStartedAt` before the daily reset boundary.
2. Run the daily reset scheduler scan with `nowMs` after the reset boundary.
3. Confirm the reset callback runs for the stale session.
4. Repeat with a fresh post-boundary session and confirm it is not reset.
5. Mark a stale session key active and confirm it is skipped.
6. Add provider-owned CLI continuity under the implicit reset policy and confirm it is skipped.
7. Change the scheduler's runtime config from a noon reset boundary to a 4 AM reset boundary and confirm the next sweep uses the new policy.

### Expected

Stale daily sessions reset without inbound traffic; fresh, active, and implicitly provider-owned CLI sessions remain unchanged; config reloads affect future scheduler sweeps.

### Actual

The focused scheduler and gateway maintenance tests match that behavior.

## Evidence

Included evidence:

- Focused scheduler and maintenance test output.
- Test and production typecheck output.
- Docs updated in:
  - `docs/concepts/session.md`
  - `docs/gateway/config-agents.md`
  - `docs/reference/session-management-compaction.md`

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: stale daily reset scheduler path, fresh-session no-op path, active-run skip, implicit provider-owned CLI skip, runtime-config reload path, gateway maintenance timer lifecycle.
- Edge cases checked: scheduler skips overlapping sweeps; shutdown/runtime cleanup clears the timer; type gates pass.
- Not verified: a live gateway instance naturally crossing the configured reset hour.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: the gateway now scans configured session stores every minute.
- Mitigation: scans are bounded to discovered session stores, skip overlapping runs, and only call reset for stale daily sessions.
- Risk: deployments that assumed lazy daily resets may observe earlier reset behavior.
- Mitigation: the new behavior matches the configured wall-clock expectation for `mode: daily` and `atHour`.
- Risk: hot config changes could otherwise make the scheduler use stale reset policy.
- Mitigation: future sweeps call the gateway runtime config getter before scanning.

commands.author: Jerry-Xin

target.title: fix(tts): defer text settlement for final-mode TTS to eliminate churn (#83511)
target.body:
## Summary

When `messages.tts.mode = "final"`, `finalizeAcpTurnOutput()` calls `settleVisibleText()` before TTS synthesis. This flushes the accumulated text to the channel, then the voice note arrives as a separate supplement — causing delete-and-resend churn on Telegram and other surfaces.

This PR defers `settleVisibleText()` until after the TTS outcome is known, and includes the visible text in the TTS payload so Telegram can deliver it as a voice-note caption.

## Root cause

```
Current main flow:
  1. settleVisibleText()     → text message sent to Telegram
  2. maybeApplyTtsToPayload  → voice note synthesized
  3. deliver(voice + supplement) → voice note sent separately
  Result: user sees text, then voice note, then text deleted = churn

Fixed flow:
  1. Skip settleVisibleText()
  2. maybeApplyTtsToPayload  → voice note synthesized
  3. deliver(voice + text as caption) → one combined message
  4. (If TTS fails → settleVisibleText() fallback)
  Result: user sees one voice note with caption = no churn
```

## Changes

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `dispatch-acp.ts` | Defer `settleVisibleText()` when `ttsMode === "final"` + include text in TTS payload + fallback on failure |
| `dispatch-acp.test.ts` | 4 new tests: deferred settlement for Telegram, eager settlement for non-caption channels, fallback on failure, immediate settlement for non-final |

## Tests

```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.test.ts
 ✓ auto-reply (54 tests) 30ms
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  54 passed (54)
```

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: `settleVisibleText()` called before TTS synthesis in `finalizeAcpTurnOutput()`, causing text-before-audio churn on Telegram.
- **Real environment tested**: Local patched OpenClaw checkout at `6de206309d`, macOS arm64, Node.js `v24.10.0`.
- **Failing proof before fix**: Applied PR test file (`dispatch-acp.test.ts` with 54 tests) to unpatched `upstream/main` (`a002c416c7`) source. 1 test fails:

```
 ❯ auto-reply dispatch-acp.test.ts (54 tests | 1 failed) 47ms

 FAIL  auto-reply > tryDispatchAcpReply > does not call settleVisibleText before TTS synthesis when mode is final
 AssertionError: expected true to be false // Object.is equality

 - Expected
 + Received

 - false
 + true

 ❯ dispatch-acp.test.ts:1928:35

 Test Files  1 failed (1)
      Tests  1 failed | 53 passed (54)
```

The failing test proves that without this patch, `settleVisibleText()` is called **before** TTS synthesis (`settleCalledBeforeTts === true`), confirming the churn bug.

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

```
git checkout origin/fix/tts-final-text-before-audio-83511
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.test.ts
```

- **Evidence after fix**:

```
 ✓ auto-reply dispatch-acp.test.ts (54 tests) 30ms
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  54 passed (54)
```

- **Observed result after fix**: All 54 tests pass including the 4 new tests covering: deferred settlement for Telegram (caption-capable channel), eager settlement for non-caption channels (Discord), text fallback when TTS synthesis fails, and immediate settlement when TTS mode is not "final".
- **What was not tested**: Live Telegram TTS session (requires TTS provider credentials and a live conversation). The settlement deferral logic is validated by 54 unit tests covering the final-mode, fallback, non-final, and channel-gating paths.

Closes #83511

target.title: fix(gateway): forward child session node events to spawnedBy subscribers
target.body:
## Summary

Describe the problem and fix in 2–5 bullets:

- Problem: gateway node subscriptions are keyed by exact `sessionKey`, so child-session node events disappear when only the parent session is subscribed.
- Why it matters: spawned agent chat activity can look dead from the gateway/node subscriber side even while work is actually running.
- What changed: added a direct-miss fallback in `createNodeSubscriptionManager.sendToSession()` that walks `spawnedBy` lineage, canonicalizes ancestor keys, and routes to the first ancestor with subscribers; added a focused regression test and changelog entry.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): this does not change ACP execution, session transcript bridging, or broader subagent progress/status surfaces.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #34852
- Related #34905

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Spawned child-session node events now reach parent-session node subscribers when only the parent session is subscribed.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation:

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS
- Runtime/container: local dev checkout
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): gateway node subscriptions
- Relevant config (redacted): default test harness

### Steps

1. Subscribe a node to a parent session key.
2. Emit a node event under a spawned child session key whose `spawnedBy` points to that parent.
3. Observe delivery through the node subscription manager.

### Expected

- Parent subscriber receives the child-session event.

### Actual

- Before this fix, delivery stopped on the child key miss and no event was sent.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: child-session events fall back through `spawnedBy` lineage to a subscribed parent; canonicalized parent lookup works; repeated sends reuse the cached ancestor lookup.
- Edge cases checked: no direct subscriber regression; partial object assertion accounts for `payloadJSON`; focused gateway test suite passes locally.
- What you did **not** verify: full gateway end-to-end node streaming against a live spawned-agent session.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps:

## Failure Recovery (if this breaks)

- How to disable/revert this change quickly: revert this PR to restore exact-key-only subscription routing.
- Files/config to restore: `src/gateway/server-node-subscriptions.ts`, `src/gateway/gateway-misc.test.ts`, `CHANGELOG.md`
- Known bad symptoms reviewers should watch for: node subscribers receiving unexpected ancestor-routed events beyond the intended `spawnedBy` chain.

## Risks and Mitigations

List only real risks for this PR. Add/remove entries as needed. If none, write `None`.

- Risk: lineage fallback could repeatedly touch session storage on hot miss paths.
  - Mitigation: fallback only runs on direct misses, uses a bounded depth, and caches the resolved ancestor subscriber key.

Supersedes the dirty branch state in #34905.

target.title: fix(memory): prevent silent vector index degradation when embedding provider temporarily unavailable
target.body:
## Bug Description

When a local embedding provider (e.g. MLX server on port 8123) is temporarily unreachable at Gateway startup, two interacting bugs cause **complete silent loss of all semantic vector data** (~11k+ chunks in our case):

### Bug 1: Promise cache deadlock in `ensureProviderInitialized()`

If `loadProviderResult()` throws (provider offline), `this.providerInitPromise` becomes a **permanently cached Rejected Promise**. The `finally` block only clears it on success (`providerInitialized === true`), so all subsequent calls immediately re-throw the stale error. The system can **never self-heal** until Gateway restart — even after the provider comes back online.

### Bug 2: Silent fts-only overwrite in `runSync()`

With `this.provider` stuck at `null`, `shouldRunFullMemoryReindex()` compares the stored `meta.model` (e.g. `jina-embeddings-v5-text-small`) against the runtime provider model. Since `provider` is null, it falls through to the `meta.model !== "fts-only"` check, returning `true`. This triggers a **full reindex** where every file is written as `fts-only`, silently erasing all existing semantic vectors.

## Fix

### Fix 1: Clear cached rejected promise on catch

In `ensureProviderInitialized()`, add a `catch` block that clears `this.providerInitPromise = null` before re-throwing. This allows subsequent calls to retry initialization when the provider comes back online (self-healing).

### Fix 2: Guard `runSync()` against silent degradation

At the top of `runSync()`, if `requestedProvider` is set and not `"none"` but the runtime `provider` is `null`, throw an error instead of proceeding. This fails loudly rather than silently degrading the entire vector index to fts-only.

## Files Changed

- `extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager.ts` — Fix 1: catch block in `ensureProviderInitialized()`
- `extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-sync-ops.ts` — Fix 2: guard clause in `runSync()`

## Real Behavior Proof

### Environment
- OpenClaw 2026.5.20 on macOS (M5, arm64)
- Local MLX embedding server (`jina-embeddings-v5-text-small`, 1024-dim) on port 8123
- Memory store: ~11,715 chunks with semantic vectors

### Steps to reproduce (before fix)
1. Stop the MLX embedding server
2. Restart the OpenClaw Gateway
3. Trigger any memory sync (search, session start, watcher)
4. Result: `shouldRunFullMemoryReindex()` returns `true`, full reindex runs with `provider === null`, all vectors silently overwritten to `fts-only`

### Verification (after fix)
1. Applied both patches to `dist/manager-D6nYRVxL.js` (production hot-fix)
2. Restarted Gateway — came up normally, `openclaw memory status --deep` shows:
   - main: 11,686 chunks, semantic vectors: **ready**, vector dims: 1024
   - codex: 11,673 chunks, semantic vectors: **ready**
3. Stopped MLX server → triggered search → sync correctly **aborted** with error:
   ```
   [A380 Shield] Embedding provider "openai" is configured but unavailable.
   Sync aborted to protect existing vector index.
   ```
4. Restarted MLX server → system **self-healed**: subsequent `ensureProviderInitialized()` succeeded, vectors intact
5. Ran `memory_search("宙狗 方案")` — returned 6 results with `vectorScore > 0.50`

### Before/After comparison
| Metric | Before fix (after MLX outage) | After fix |
|--------|------|------|
| Semantic vectors | **erased** (fts-only) | preserved (11,715 chunks) |
| Self-healing | ❌ requires manual Gateway restart | ✅ auto-recovers when provider returns |
| Failure mode | silent data loss | loud error, data protected |

## Checklist

- [x] Bug fix (non-breaking change)
- [x] Tested locally on production instance
- [x] No CHANGELOG.md changes (maintainer entry)
- [ ] CI passing (awaiting)

target.body:
## Summary
- add a shared scoped mention-pattern policy for global, agent, and provider/channel overrides
- apply configured mention-pattern gating across channel runtimes while preserving native mentions
- document raw regex-body syntax and regenerate config/help metadata

## Notes
- Recreated from a clean `origin/main` branch after #70846 was closed as dirty; this branch has been rebased on current `origin/main` and generated baselines refreshed after upstream movement.

## Validation
- `pnpm --config.minimum-release-age=0 install --lockfile-only --ignore-scripts=false --config.engine-strict=false --config.enable-pre-post-scripts=true --config.side-effects-cache=true`
- `pnpm plugin-sdk:api:check`
- `pnpm config:channels:check`
- `pnpm test extensions/qa-channel/src/inbound.test.ts src/channels/mention-pattern-policy.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-context.body.test.ts`
- `pnpm check:changed`
- `git diff --check`

## Real behavior proof
- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Scoped mention-pattern policy now resolves the effective provider/agent/conversation policy so configured regex mentions are enabled only for explicitly allowed conversations in deny mode and remain disabled for denied or unlisted conversations. Latest follow-up rebased onto current `origin/main`, resolved refreshed generated config/plugin SDK baseline conflicts, and reran the exact-head behavior proof.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw PR checkout on host `patrick`, branch `feature/mention-pattern-policy-clean`, head `c4a2be36ad7dd3a6d544a4dab693efb978f18494`, running the real source resolver, Telegram group peer id helper, mention matcher, and inbound mention decision code through Node/tsx against redacted OpenClaw-style config/message facts. This does not exercise live external delivery.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** `cd /home/patrick/projects/openclaw-mention-pattern-policy && HEAD_SHA=c4a2be36ad7dd3a6d544a4dab693efb978f18494 HEAD_SHORT=c4a2be36ad node --import tsx /tmp/pr70864-proof-02d093.mjs`
- **Evidence after fix:** Terminal output from the real source smoke:

```text
OpenClaw scoped mention policy Telegram account media pre-download proof (c4a2be36ad, c4a2be36ad7dd3a6d544a4dab693efb978f18494)
{"topic":7,"conversationId":"-1001234567893:topic:7","accountPolicyAllowIn":["-1001234567893:topic:7"],"globalProviderAllowIn":["-1001234567893:topic:global-only"],"mentionPatternsEnabled":true,"wasMentioned":true,"shouldSkip":false,"skipReason":null}
{"topic":9,"conversationId":"-1001234567893:topic:9","accountPolicyAllowIn":["-1001234567893:topic:7"],"globalProviderAllowIn":["-1001234567893:topic:global-only"],"mentionPatternsEnabled":false,"wasMentioned":false,"shouldSkip":true,"skipReason":null}
{"topic":11,"conversationId":"-1001234567893:topic:11","accountPolicyAllowIn":["-1001234567893:topic:7"],"globalProviderAllowIn":["-1001234567893:topic:global-only"],"mentionPatternsEnabled":false,"wasMentioned":false,"shouldSkip":true,"skipReason":null}
```

- **Observed result after fix:** In inherited `deny` mode, the media pre-download path honors the Telegram account policy rather than the global provider fallback: account-allowed topic `-1001234567893:topic:7` enables configured regex mention `bot` and media pre-download is not skipped, while account-denied topic `:topic:9` and unlisted topic `:topic:11` disable configured regex mentions and skip unaddressed group media before download.
- **What was not tested:** Full live external Telegram delivery was not exercised in this proof; CI and targeted channel tests cover the integration paths.

target.title: fix(gateway): run daily session resets on schedule
target.body:
## Summary

- Daily session resets now run from gateway maintenance instead of waiting for the next inbound session use.
- The scheduler scans configured session stores and calls the existing reset service for stale daily sessions after the configured boundary.
- The maintenance timer now reads current runtime config on each sweep, so hot config changes to `session.reset`, `session.resetByType`, `session.resetByChannel`, or store targets are applied without a gateway restart.
- Docs now describe proactive daily rollover and the implicit provider-owned CLI session exemption.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [x] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [x] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [x] CI/CD / infra
- [x] Docs

## Linked Issue/PR

- Fixes #82321
- Related: N/A
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Motivation

A daily reset with `atHour` is a wall-clock policy. Users should not need to send a new message after the reset boundary before stale session context is cleared. The maintenance sweep should also honor the current runtime config after hot reloads, not the startup snapshot.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Daily sessions stale past `atHour` are reset by gateway maintenance without waiting for a new inbound message, while active runs and implicit provider-owned CLI sessions are preserved.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout in a Linux container, on branch `fix-daily-session-reset`, running the patched gateway maintenance timer against an isolated temp `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`. Bundled plugin facades were disabled for the proof process with `OPENCLAW_DISABLE_BUNDLED_PLUGINS=1` so the reset service did not depend on source-checkout plugin artifacts.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - Ran a live Node command that starts `startGatewayMaintenanceTimers` with the patched daily reset scheduler, current-config getter, real `performGatewaySessionReset` path, isolated temp session store, one stale daily session, one stale active-run session, and one implicit provider-owned CLI session.
  - Command shape: `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=<tmp> OPENCLAW_DISABLE_BUNDLED_PLUGINS=1 node --import tsx - <<'TS' ... startGatewayMaintenanceTimers(...) ... TS`
  - Supplemental verification: `./node_modules/.bin/vitest run src/gateway/session-daily-reset-scheduler.test.ts src/gateway/server-maintenance.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway.config.ts`
  - Supplemental type gates: `pnpm check:test-types && pnpm tsgo:prod`
- Evidence after fix:
  ```text
  $ OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=<tmp> OPENCLAW_DISABLE_BUNDLED_PLUGINS=1 node --import tsx - <<'TS'
  # creates isolated agents/main/sessions/sessions.json
  # starts patched gateway maintenance timers
  # waits for daily reset sweep to invoke the real reset service
  TS
  gateway-proof: before {"stale":"old-regular-session","active":"old-active-session","cli":"old-cli-session"}
  [session-daily-reset] daily session reset sweep complete
  gateway-proof: after {"stale":"5f959c12-f0ed-4ed1-99d2-cc158c66ac87","active":"old-active-session","cli":"old-cli-session"}
  gateway-proof: staleReset true
  gateway-proof: activePreserved true
  gateway-proof: implicitCliPreserved true

  $ ./node_modules/.bin/vitest run src/gateway/session-daily-reset-scheduler.test.ts src/gateway/server-maintenance.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway.config.ts
  Test Files  2 passed (2)
  Tests       18 passed (18)

  $ pnpm check:test-types && pnpm tsgo:prod
  exit_code=0
  ```
- Observed result after fix: The running gateway maintenance timer reset the stale daily session through the actual reset service without any inbound message. The active-run session and implicit provider-owned CLI session kept their original session IDs. Focused coverage also verifies fresh sessions are left alone and later sweeps use current runtime config after reset policy changes.
- What was not tested: A multi-hour live gateway wait that naturally crosses the configured reset hour.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

Daily reset freshness was only evaluated through session-use paths. If no message arrived after `atHour`, the stale session could remain active even though the configured reset time had passed. The initial scheduler also captured the startup config, which meant hot reloads could leave the sweep using stale reset policy until restart.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level:
  - [ ] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file:
  - `src/gateway/session-daily-reset-scheduler.test.ts`
  - `src/gateway/server-maintenance.test.ts`
- Locked-in scenarios:
  - A stale daily session resets during a scheduler sweep without a new message.
  - A fresh daily session is left alone.
  - Implicit provider-owned CLI sessions are not cut by the default daily policy.
  - Sessions with active runs are skipped.
  - Scheduler sweeps use current runtime config after a reset-policy change.
- Existing coverage before this patch covered stale detection when called, but not proactive scheduled execution or reload-aware maintenance behavior.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Configured daily session resets now happen on schedule during gateway uptime, even if no message arrives after the reset boundary. The public docs now state that proactive rollover can happen from gateway maintenance and that implicit provider-owned CLI sessions keep continuity unless reset is explicit.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
daily reset boundary
  -> gateway maintenance sweep
  -> read current runtime config
  -> skip active runs and implicit provider-owned CLI sessions
  -> stale daily sessions reset through existing reset service
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- No security-impacting changes identified.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux container
- Runtime/container: Local OpenClaw checkout
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel: N/A
- Relevant config: `session.reset.mode=daily`, `session.reset.atHour=4`

### Steps

1. Create a session store entry with `sessionStartedAt` before the daily reset boundary.
2. Run the daily reset scheduler scan with `nowMs` after the reset boundary.
3. Confirm the reset callback runs for the stale session.
4. Repeat with a fresh post-boundary session and confirm it is not reset.
5. Mark a stale session key active and confirm it is skipped.
6. Add provider-owned CLI continuity under the implicit reset policy and confirm it is skipped.
7. Change the scheduler's runtime config from a noon reset boundary to a 4 AM reset boundary and confirm the next sweep uses the new policy.

### Expected

Stale daily sessions reset without inbound traffic; fresh, active, and implicitly provider-owned CLI sessions remain unchanged; config reloads affect future scheduler sweeps.

### Actual

The focused scheduler and gateway maintenance tests match that behavior.

## Evidence

Included evidence:

- Focused scheduler and maintenance test output.
- Test and production typecheck output.
- Docs updated in:
  - `docs/concepts/session.md`
  - `docs/gateway/config-agents.md`
  - `docs/reference/session-management-compaction.md`

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: stale daily reset scheduler path, fresh-session no-op path, active-run skip, implicit provider-owned CLI skip, runtime-config reload path, gateway maintenance timer lifecycle.
- Edge cases checked: scheduler skips overlapping sweeps; shutdown/runtime cleanup clears the timer; type gates pass.
- Not verified: a live gateway instance naturally crossing the configured reset hour.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: the gateway now scans configured session stores every minute.
- Mitigation: scans are bounded to discovered session stores, skip overlapping runs, and only call reset for stale daily sessions.
- Risk: deployments that assumed lazy daily resets may observe earlier reset behavior.
- Mitigation: the new behavior matches the configured wall-clock expectation for `mode: daily` and `atHour`.
- Risk: hot config changes could otherwise make the scheduler use stale reset policy.
- Mitigation: future sweeps call the gateway runtime config getter before scanning.

target.title: fix(commitments): stop hardcoding fastMode in extraction (#78451)
target.body:
## Summary

MiniMax Coding Plan operators whose plan owns `MiniMax-M2.7` but not the `-highspeed` variant hit `HTTP 500: your current token plan not support model, MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed (2061)` on every commitment extraction pass, because the commitments extraction path passed `fastMode: true` unconditionally to `runEmbeddedPiAgent`, which caused the MiniMax stream wrapper's `createMinimaxFastModeWrapper` to rewrite the base model id to `MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed`.

## Root Cause

- `src/commitments/runtime.ts:242` (pre-fix): the call to `runEmbeddedPiAgent` for hidden commitment extraction hardcoded `fastMode: true`.
- `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/minimax-stream-wrappers.ts:24`: the MiniMax stream wrapper accepts a `fastMode` boolean and, when true, swaps the base model id to its `-highspeed` variant.
- Result: every commitments extraction request silently flipped `MiniMax-M2.7` → `MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed`, which Coding Plan operators do not own. MiniMax responds with HTTP 500 / error code 2061. Reporter verified the Coding Plan owns the base model but not highspeed.
- The reporter listed three valid fix shapes (drop the hardcode, add a config field, or inherit agent fastMode). This PR uses option 1: dropping the hardcode lets the existing `resolveFastModeState` helper continue to control fast-mode for the rest of the agent runtime, and commitments extraction inherits the same default-false behavior.

## Changes

- `src/commitments/runtime.ts`: remove `fastMode: true` from the `runEmbeddedPiAgent` payload built in the commitments extraction path. Add a comment explaining the reason so this does not regress.
- `src/commitments/runtime.test.ts`: extend the existing `runEmbeddedPiAgentMock` assertion to assert `fastMode` is **not** in the payload, locking in the regression fix.
- `CHANGELOG.md`: single-line entry under Unreleased / Fixes.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: On every commitment extraction pass, MiniMax Coding Plan operators receive `HTTP 500: your current token plan not support model, MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed (2061)` from the MiniMax server because the agent runtime requested `MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed` even though their plan only owns `MiniMax-M2.7`. The reporter (`@advaitpaliwal`) verified `commitments.enabled: true, commitments.maxPerDay: 10` config, then ran a real Coding Plan session, observed extraction always selecting the highspeed variant, and traced the root cause to `dist/runtime-CXt7BFiH.js` line 386 (`fastMode: true` hardcoded). Issue: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/78451
- **Real environment tested**: Local OpenClaw checkout at `../openclaw-78451` on Windows 11 + Node 22.14. The smoke script reads the **patched production file `src/commitments/runtime.ts` directly from the worktree**, locates the entire `defaultExtractBatch` function (the path that builds the `runEmbeddedPiAgent` payload), prints its SHA-256 + verbatim bytes, then runs structural assertions over the captured payload literal to prove `fastMode: true` is gone, the `#78451` explanatory comment is in place, and every sibling field is preserved. No mocks, no test runner — the patched source bytes drive the smoke. PR head: `a3189f329687fa8c2c9da783afbc0d644ea94573`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: `git checkout fix/commitments-no-fastmode-hardcode && node --experimental-strip-types ./smoke-pr78459.mts`, where `smoke-pr78459.mts` reads `src/commitments/runtime.ts`, slices the `async function defaultExtractBatch` body, hashes it, prints the verbatim payload object literal, and runs structural assertions against the live patched source.
- **Evidence after fix**: Terminal output captured locally on PR head `a3189f3296` (Windows 11 + Node 22.14, the patched `src/commitments/runtime.ts` `defaultExtractBatch` function read from the worktree, 1660 bytes, SHA-256 `ae3690fc3189a7bc3e6d33e8693a3a28aa24ee3c7a82822618c5f1c762bcb379`).

~~~text
$ node --experimental-strip-types ./smoke-pr78459.mts
=== Patched defaultExtractBatch function — bytes from src/commitments/runtime.ts ===
offset: 7245 -> 8905 ( 1660 bytes)
SHA-256: ae3690fc3189a7bc3e6d33e8693a3a28aa24ee3c7a82822618c5f1c762bcb379

function body still contains literal 'fastMode: true': false
explanatory '#78451' comment present                : true
  preserved field 'disableTools: true': true
  preserved field 'thinkLevel: "off"': true
  preserved field 'verboseLevel: "off"': true
  preserved field 'reasoningLevel: "off"': true

=== Payload object passed to runEmbeddedPiAgent (verbatim from patched code) ===
{ cfg, agentId: first.agentId });
  const result = await runEmbeddedPiAgent({
    sessionId: runId,
    sessionKey: `agent:${first.agentId}:commitments:${runId}`,
    agentId: first.agentId,
    trigger: "manual",
    sessionFile: resolveExtractionSessionFile(first.agentId, runId),
    workspaceDir: resolveAgentWorkspaceDir(cfg, first.agentId),
    config: cfg,
    provider: modelRef.provider,
    model: modelRef.model,
    prompt: buildCommitmentExtractionPrompt({ cfg, items: params.items }),
    disableTools: true,
    thinkLevel: "off",
    verboseLevel: "off",
    reasoningLevel: "off",
    // Do not hardcode fastMode here: leaving the field undefined lets the
    // commitments extraction inherit the configured fast-mode state (which
    // defaults to false). Hardcoding true caused MiniMax Coding Plans without
    // highspeed access to fail with HTTP 500 because the MiniMax stream wrapper
    // would rewrite MiniMax-M2.7 -> MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed (#78451).
    timeoutMs: resolved.extraction.timeoutSeconds * 1000,
    runId,
    bootstrapContextMode: "lightweight",
    skillsSnapshot: { prompt: "", skills: [] },
    suppressToolErrorWarnings: true,
  }

=== fastMode-related lines in the payload (BEFORE-vs-AFTER comparison) ===
  // Do not hardcode fastMode here: leaving the field undefined lets the
  // highspeed access to fail with HTTP 500 because the MiniMax stream wrapper
  // would rewrite MiniMax-M2.7 -> MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed (#78451).
~~~

- **Observed result after fix**: The patched `defaultExtractBatch` function — pinned by a 1660-byte / SHA-256 `ae3690fc3189a7bc3e6d33e8693a3a28aa24ee3c7a82822618c5f1c762bcb379` integrity check — has zero `fastMode: true` literals (`function body still contains literal 'fastMode: true': false`). The explanatory `#78451` comment is in place (`explanatory '#78451' comment present: true`), and every sibling field (`disableTools: true`, `thinkLevel: "off"`, `verboseLevel: "off"`, `reasoningLevel: "off"`) is preserved verbatim. The captured payload literal shown verbatim above confirms `runEmbeddedPiAgent` is now called without a `fastMode` key — so the MiniMax stream wrapper at `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/minimax-stream-wrappers.ts:24` no longer flips `MiniMax-M2.7` → `MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed`, and the operator's configured base model id flows through unchanged.
- **What was not tested**: A live HTTP roundtrip against the real MiniMax server with a Coding Plan token is not available on the contributor machine, so this is source-level / structural proof against the patched payload-builder, not a live MiniMax 200 response. Maintainers may apply `proof: override` if a live MiniMax recording is required; the reporter's original issue already contains live HTTP 500 evidence from the real Coding Plan.

## Test

- `pnpm test src/commitments/runtime.test.ts` — 6 passed (including the new `expect(callArgs).not.toHaveProperty("fastMode")` regression assertion).

## Notes

- Scope: smallest complete fix. No new config surface and no provider/wrapper edits — the existing fast-mode resolution chain already covers the right behavior; the hardcode was the only thing forcing `true`.
- Compatibility: agents that need fast-mode for commitments can still set it via `agents.defaults.models.<id>.params.fastMode: true` (or per-agent `fastModeDefault: true`) — the `resolveFastModeState` chain is unchanged.

Closes #78451

target.title: fix(discord): keep forced voice consult diagnostics private
target.body:
## Summary
- Stop forced Discord realtime consult fallbacks from appending internal consult-policy diagnostics to the agent-facing voice prompt.
- Apply the same private-diagnostic treatment to phone-call realtime forced consults while preserving the forced-consult reason in server debug logs.
- Add Discord voice regression coverage and update voice-call expectations; add an Unreleased changelog entry.

## Verification
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs --config test/vitest/vitest.e2e.config.ts extensions/discord/src/voice/manager.e2e.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-voice-call.config.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-voice-call.config.ts extensions/voice-call/src/webhook/realtime-handler.test.ts --reporter=verbose -t "forces an agent consult from final user transcript when consult policy is always"`
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check extensions/discord/src/voice/realtime.ts extensions/voice-call/src/webhook/realtime-handler.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `AUTOREVIEW_AUTO_TESTS=0 .agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: Realtime voice forced consult fallback no longer feeds internal consult-policy diagnostics into model-facing prompts that can be spoken back to users.
Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout with mocked Discord realtime voice and voice-call realtime provider harnesses.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran the Vitest commands listed above after moving the patch onto a clean branch from origin/main; after the review-comment fix, reran the Discord e2e file, the focused voice-call forced-consult regression, oxfmt check, and `git diff --check`.
Evidence after fix: Discord regression asserts the agent command message is exactly `What?` and does not contain `consultPolicy` or `openclaw_agent_consult`; voice-call tests assert forced consult args include only the caller question; both forced fallback paths now log the private forced-consult reason through server debug logging instead of passing it to the model/tool payload.
Observed result after fix: Discord voice e2e file passed; voice-call forced-consult regression passed; diff whitespace check passed; touched files are oxfmt-clean; autoreview reported no accepted/actionable findings on the original patch.
What was not tested: A live Discord or phone call with real OpenAI realtime audio was not run.

target.title: fix(feishu): use union_id as fallback for @mention detection in multi-bot groups
target.body:
## Problem

Fixes #40768

When multiple Feishu bots share the same group, @mentioning a specific bot is not recognized. The bot logs `message in group ... did not mention bot` even though it's correctly mentioned.

## Root Cause

Feishu `open_id` is **app-scoped** — the same bot has a different `open_id` depending on which app's token is used to query it. Confirmed by querying the same message with two different bot tokens:

```
# Same @mention, different app contexts:
writer token → mention open_id: ou_4ede388c79638a9057a432c3e3832f91
tron token   → mention open_id: ou_04dc13327d491d9e36da57826a4131f0
```

WebSocket push events deliver mention `open_id`s in a specific app context that may not match the receiving bot's own `open_id`, causing `checkBotMentioned()` to always return `false`.

## Fix

`union_id` is globally unique across all apps for the same entity — it doesn't vary by app context. This PR:

1. Fetches `union_id` alongside `open_id` from `bot/v3/info` at startup
2. Stores it in a new `botUnionIds` map in monitor state
3. Passes it through to `checkBotMentioned()` as a fallback
4. Matches mentions by `union_id` when `open_id` doesn't match

The `open_id` match is preserved as the primary check; `union_id` is a fallback, so existing behavior is unchanged for single-bot setups.

## Files Changed

- `types.ts`: add `botUnionId` to `FeishuProbeResult`
- `probe.ts`: extract `union_id` from `bot/v3/info` response
- `monitor.startup.ts`: propagate `botUnionId` through `FeishuMonitorBotIdentity`
- `monitor.state.ts`: add `botUnionIds` map with proper clear/delete
- `monitor.account.ts`: store/clear `botUnionId`, pass to `handleFeishuMessage`
- `bot.ts`: accept `botUnionId` in `checkBotMentioned` and `parseFeishuMessageEvent`, match by `union_id` when `open_id` mismatches

target.title: fix(update): avoid broad tag fetches for dev updates
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: `openclaw update --channel dev` could fail before updating `main` when a release tag on the remote conflicted with an existing local tag.
- Solution: make dev updates fetch branches/remotes with `--no-tags`, while keeping release-channel tag fetching unchanged.
- What changed: explicit dev tag targets now fetch only the requested tag from configured remotes before resolving it.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): stable/beta updates remain tag-driven and still fetch tags broadly.

## Motivation

- This fixes the observed beta-to-dev switch failure where an unrelated moved beta tag blocked a dev update that should track git `main`.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: `openclaw update --channel dev` no longer performs a broad tag fetch that can fail on unrelated moved release tags before updating from `main`.
- Real environment tested: Local bare git remote plus clean checkout reproducer, using real `git fetch` and `git rebase` commands with a moved conflicting `v2026.5.19-beta.2` tag.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Created a local bare remote, cloned it, moved the remote beta tag, confirmed current-main-style `git fetch --all --prune --tags` fails, then confirmed PR-style `git fetch --all --prune --no-tags` followed by `git rebase origin/main` succeeds.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):

```text
proof_root=<temp-redacted>

old dev broad tag fetch (current main behavior):
exit=1
From <temp-redacted>/remote
   c39fe2e..63b8b7c  main              -> origin/main
 ! [rejected]        v2026.5.19-beta.2 -> v2026.5.19-beta.2  (would clobber existing tag)

reset checkout to before proof fetch:
clean_status=0

new dev fetch + rebase (PR behavior):
local_tag_before=c39fe2e
remote_tag_after=63b8b7c
local_tag_after_no_tags_fetch=c39fe2e
head_matches_origin_main_after_rebase=yes

release-channel broad tag fetch remains tag-sensitive:
exit=1
From <temp-redacted>/remote
 ! [rejected]        v2026.5.19-beta.2 -> v2026.5.19-beta.2  (would clobber existing tag)
```

Supplemental focused test proof:

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/update-runner.test.ts
Test Files 1 passed (1)
Tests 46 passed (46)
```

- Observed result after fix: The real git reproducer shows the old broad tag fetch fails with the same `would clobber existing tag` class of error, while the PR-style dev fetch avoids tag mutation and the checkout rebases to `origin/main`. A broad tag fetch still fails afterward, preserving the proof that release-channel tag-driven behavior remains tag-sensitive.
- What was not tested: A live openclaw-prod update was not run from this agent.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): User-reported failure showed `openclaw update --channel dev` rejected `v2026.5.19-beta.2` during broad tag fetch with `would clobber existing tag`.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: Dev updates used the same broad tag-fetch behavior as release-channel updates even though dev is branch/main based.
- Missing detection / guardrail: Tests covered dev update fetch failures but did not assert that dev avoids broad tag fetching.
- Contributing context (if known): Switching from beta to dev can leave local release tags around, and Git rejects clobbering changed tags unless forced.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `src/infra/update-runner.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: Dev updates fetch with `--no-tags`; explicit dev tag targets fetch only the requested tag; failed targeted tag fetches do not fall back to stale local tags.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: The update runner already injects the git command runner, so these tests verify the exact command contract without needing a mutable remote fixture.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): Existing dev update tests covered fetch sequencing but not the tag-fetch mode.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

`openclaw update --channel dev` now ignores unrelated remote tags during the normal branch update path. Explicit dev tag targets still fetch the requested tag.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
openclaw update --channel dev -> git fetch --all --prune --tags -> unrelated tag conflict -> update fails

After:
openclaw update --channel dev -> git fetch --all --prune --no-tags -> rebase main -> update continues
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: The git fetch command changes for dev updates only. It narrows normal dev fetch behavior by suppressing tag fetches; explicit tag targets fetch only the requested tag ref.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux worktree
- Runtime/container: Node via repo test wrapper; local git bare remote proof
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): update CLI git mode
- Relevant config (redacted): N/A

### Steps

1. Create a local bare git remote and clone it.
2. Create local tag `v2026.5.19-beta.2`, then move the remote tag to a newer commit.
3. Run current-main-style `git fetch --all --prune --tags` and observe tag-clobber failure.
4. Reset the checkout to clean state, run PR-style `git fetch --all --prune --no-tags`, then `git rebase origin/main`.
5. Run `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/update-runner.test.ts`.

### Expected

- Current-main-style broad tag fetch fails on the moved tag.
- PR-style dev fetch does not mutate/fetch the moved tag and branch rebase succeeds.
- Stable/beta paths continue to use tag fetching.
- Explicit dev tag targets do not use a stale local tag after targeted tag fetch failure.

### Actual

- Real git proof: broad tag fetch failed with `would clobber existing tag`; PR-style dev fetch plus rebase succeeded with `head_matches_origin_main_after_rebase=yes`.
- `src/infra/update-runner.test.ts` passed 46/46.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: real local bare-remote moved-tag reproduction, targeted update-runner tests, `git diff --check`, and `codex review --base origin/main` after rebase.
- Edge cases checked: default dev update fetch mode, explicit dev tag target, failed targeted tag fetch with a stale local tag candidate, release-channel broad tag sensitivity.
- What you did **not** verify: live update on openclaw-prod.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: Explicit dev tag targets could otherwise resolve stale local tags after a failed targeted fetch.
  - Mitigation: The targeted tag fetch is forced for the requested ref and local tag resolution is skipped when all configured remote fetches fail.

## Summary
- route the visible WebChat attachment button to the file input inside its own composer instead of using a document-wide query
- mark the toolbar control as type=button so it cannot accidentally submit surrounding forms
- add coverage for the visible button-to-hidden-input activation path

## Verification

- node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/views/chat.test.ts
- pnpm check:changed
- git diff --check

## Not Run
- Live Chrome/Windows native file-picker smoke; this environment is headless/Linux.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: WebChat's visible attachment button must trigger the hidden file input in the same `.agent-chat__input` composer, not the first `.agent-chat__file-input` in the document. This covers issue #47983 in `ui/src/ui/views/chat.ts`.
- Real environment tested: Local Linux 6.19.14+kali-amd64 checkout of PR head `b0f962a4b1`; Node v22.22.2; pnpm 11.1.0 via corepack. DOM smoke used Lit + jsdom with the actual `renderChat(...)` implementation from `ui/src/ui/views/chat.ts`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  1. Created an untracked DOM smoke script under `.artifacts/proof-83952/` that imports `ui/src/ui/views/chat.ts`, renders two WebChat composers, stubs both hidden file inputs' `.click()`, then clicks the second visible attachment button.
